title: Shrill sounding with no bass.
body: While I'm not an audiophile, I DO know good sound when I hear it so trust me.. these things are really anemic in the "bottom end". The 9mm drivers, 20hz-20Khz range and 97dB/mW specs for these ErgoFits are pretty standard for good sounding buds so I assume the problem must be with Panasonic designing the speaker outlets on these as OVALS instead of round in the interest of more ergos and comfort.. Well, they should've left it alone because they're not any more comfortable and tbh, are closer to screetchy nails on a chalkboard than anything with "hi-fidelity" :-/By the way, it has NOTHING to do with which size adapter is installed for "making a better seal", etc. See, since these Panasonic ErgoFits and some other buds called JVC Riptidz were both under six bucks, I got both and just tried them side-by-side. It was like night and day!To those that feel the bass on these is adequate, it may be because you don't have another set for an immediate relative comparison, or it's just a matter of taste where bass isn't a priority.I'm not surprised the Riptidz blew these out of the water because they've got bigger drivers, a wider freq range and higher output overall.JVC Riptidz: 11mm drivers, 10hz-20Khz range, 101dB/mW output.Panasonic ErgoFit: 9mm drivers, 20hz-20Khz range, 97dB/mW output.It's just a matter of physics and the numbers don't lie: the Riptidz deliver more quantity of low end, while the mids and highs were more similar. But even for songs (or parts of songs) where the low end wasn't prevalent, the ErgoFits seemed shrill, cold and lifeless in comparison while the Riptidz were better balanced tonally.I probably wouldn't have been so harsh on these Ergofits if I didn't have the Riptidz for a relative side-by-side comparison, so you may actually find these, "OK".To sum up, I'd advise steering clear of these and going with the JVC's or you may find yourself fiddling with ear adapters and your EQ in some futile hunt for all the missing BASS. And you won't find it because Panasonic killed it in their R&D lab with these stupid "oval holes" lol.

title: Horrible. So much for reviews.
body: What is wrong with these people? Never before had the reviews lied, but I don't know what has gotten into these other reviewers this time. I actualy am starting to wonder if Panasonic has planted moles to write good reviews for this product. They clearly have never listened to music in their lives. I would give this 0 stars if that we're possible.These earbuds have absolutely no bass whatsoever, and on top of that, they're extremely tinny. I didn't think there could be anything you could buy that has bad value that was under $7 as long as it worked, but this proved me wrong. Listening to the Tron soundtrack, which is very bass heavy, it sounded almost foreign, as you could not hear many of the important parts of the music (because they are bassy notes). Listening to "Another One Bites the Dust" by Queen (I think that's the name), I couldn't hear the whole first 20 seconds or so before the singing came in, aside from some very subtle tingling feelings in my ear where the the bass guitar plays, and I recently took a hearing test and was found to have average hearing, so that wasn't the problem.I know I only payed $7, and you get what you pay for, but I wouldn't pay more than about $1.50 for these, maybe even less. The whole reason I bought these was to replace the cheap $3 ones I got off of eBay that kept breaking after about 2 months each time, but to tell you the truth, even the $3 ones put these to shame. I thought that I might as well spend an extra few bucks to get a name brand that would at least last, but these are horrible. Words cannot express my hatred for these earbuds.These earbuds will be sent back, and the $7 will be spent on RCA earbuds that I have used in the past and I know to be of great quality.

title: Finally! Earbuds that don't hurt my ears.
body: EDIT: These worked for less than 1 day. I tried them out right after they arrived, the sound good and they were very comfortable to wear. I watched half of a movie on my laptop, switched them to my MP3 and walked 3 miles, did a little stretching then put them on my dresser...the next morning I started my MP3 and the sound in the left ear kept going in and out, I checked to make sure the jack was plugged in all the way and as soon as I touched it the sound came back but when I let go it went out in the left ear again. I'm an adult, not hard on the things I have (I've had the same Sony MP3 player for 4 years and it's going strong) and I know better than to pull on the cord when removing the buds from my ears so obviously I'm not impressed with the durability will be asking Amazon for a refund.YAY! Finally a pair of earbuds that don't hurt my ears... Not sure why it is, maybe I have small ears, but I frequently have problems with earbuds hurting after wearing them for only a minute or two. I haven't had any problem with these hurting even when wearing the for long periods of time, in fact they are really comfortable, and so far they are staying put in my ears when I'm doing busy with more energetic activities (think biking, yoga, vacuuming, race walking, etc.). Sound is good (although I'm no audiophile), clear without any undesirable static noises and the bass sounds good to me. I can't speak for their ability to stand up to daily use as I haven't had them long, but if they fail I'll come back with an update. I gave them 4 stars because they are so far as expected and haven't disappointed me. I rarely give 5 stars, only if something performs well above and beyond expectations so a 4 star rating is high in my book.

title: Disappointed with the fit after reading all the great reviews
body: I bought these because they have great reviews and people say they're great for running, but I have found the opposite to be the case. Maybe there's something wrong with my ears, but I've tried all the inserts and there's no way these things stay in my ear.I run almost daily and just want something for background noise and to hear Runkeeper. I have used LG Tone (http://www.amazon.com/LG-Electronics-HBS-730-Bluetooth-Headset/dp/B009A5204K/) in the past and although they're definitely not made for running, they at least stay in much longer than these Panasonics. Where as I'd have to adjust/put the LG's back in my ears maybe every 1/4 mile, the Panasonics come out every 100-200 meters. I got so frustrated with them, I just pulled them out and listened to the speaker on my phone instead.I wasn't as concerned with sound quality as I was with them just staying in place. However, the sound quality is kind of hit or miss. Some songs sound good, but most content is just "ok" with somewhat boosted highs and missing lows. A problem I don't have with my Sennheiser headphones (which couldn't ever be used for running). I'm not one of those people who likes the smile curve (boosted lows and highs); I like a flat frequency response and these headphones sound like the highs are boosted and the bass is missing. You wouldn't listen to rap in these. They do a decent job with good quality music, but you get what you pay for - don't expect miracles.Back to running: so far the only headphones that I've tried that stay in my ear well during the runs are Yurbuds (I have two different types), but I plan on trying a few more, at which point I'll update my review with more info.

title: Listening to music on these was painful, in more ways than one.
body: Really disappointed in these after reading so many great reviews. I'm always searching for great-sounding yet affordable in-ear headphones. I've never used a pair of headphones that cost over $15, so I can't imagine I'm expecting too much. Here's what I found:Sound quality: Highly disappointed. I switched back and forth between these and a couple of other sub-$15 headphones while listening to a couple tracks that I know well and that have a wide range of sounds represented. The Panasonics had noticeably less volume, an uncomfortably crisp treble, and hardly any low or low-mid to speak of. This is AFTER fiddling around with the different sized earpieces and forcing a good seal. Switching back from these to my Skullcandy Jibs ($10) was like taking a breath of fresh air: night and day difference. Even putting in a pair of $5 UPS store in-ears after the Panasonics was refreshing.Comfort: These may have been the most uncomfortable in-ear headphones I've ever tried. I felt like the hard plastic was cutting into the inside of my ear, and any movement only made it worse. It also felt strange and unusual to have the "marshmellow" earpieces so off-center. A good seal was difficult and required quite a bit of fiddling, and wasn't even rewarded with very good sound. Again, it was immediately refreshing to switch from these to any of my other pairs of cheap headphones.About the only good thing I have to say about them is that they come in some nice colors and have a right-angle jack, which I actually appreciate.I'd still much rather have a $5 pair of hideous UPS Store headphones over these any day though.

title: Confused? why are these 4.5 stars?
body: Ok, lately I realized that the type of headphones you use obviously impacts the quality and enjoyment of your music. I know it's an obvious statement but most of us just make do with the standard set of headphones you get with you iPod or MP3 player. Over the last 2 years I've been searching for cheap, great qulaity and sound headphones. At work I use Sennheiser HD202 - cost $25 at Bestbuy and they are awesome! I've also had Beats, Sony, JVC, but the HD202's are amazing for the price. I needed ear buds for the gym so here I am.I bought the iLuv iEP322RED at the same time I bought these Panosonics. They are both about $6.I listen to a lot of music, from Depeche Mode to AC/DC to Gregorian Chant. I believe my hearing to be good and there's nothing unusual about my ears LOLThe 2 sets of headphones arrived at the same time (took 5 days for shipping). I compared them both, switching them out during the same songs. The iLuvs won hands down! The quality of music is far superior to these Panosonics. The iLuv's are a much better fit (and I tried repositioning the headphones and using the different sizes of buds. The iLuvs are louder with deeper bass. These Panosonics are weak and you have to turn the volume high to get a decent response. I don't know how there are thousands of reviews for these headphones and it shows an average of 4.5 stars? Maybe people just bought these and didn't compare? For $6 they are a nice set and do the job but there are many out there in this price range and for me the iLuvs are far far better.Good Luck!

title: Didn't work as they should.
body: Was given these as a present - wife bought them based on the overall good reviews, but they didnt work at all for me.Now, to be fair, in-ear is not my favorite style of headphones, but that wasn't the issue, they were actually fairly comfortable. The issue is the sound isn't consistent and was terrible. When I could get it to be consistently stay plugged in properly, sound was fairly good. My suspicion is the plug isn't built properly - any slight movement of the wire, my phone, etc. would cause the sound to be bad - only in one ear, volume would go down, remote wouldn't work, etc. Would go in an out, especially if I put my phone in my pocket and was walking, which I often do. I'm pretty sure the plug wasn't staying in the headphone jack properly - maybe it was too small? I thought maybe my phone case was doing something (i.e. too thick and was 'pulling' the cord out), but tried without the case and had the same issue.On another note, couldn't figure out how to use the remote on the wire to change the volume - maybe I was missing how to use it for that, but I'm usually pretty good with this stuff, so pretty sure there wasn't a way to change the volume with it - if this was the case, it is a bad design.I really wanted to like these headphones, but at least the pair I have just don't work like they should. I'm going to try to replace these, as I'm hoping that perhaps I just got a defective pair. Hopeful that a new pair will be better, but not entirely convinced....if a new pair works better, I plan to update this review.

title: Failed me for the first time after 3 buys
body: Other types of earphones fall right out of my ears, but I always buy this exact pair of earphones because I like how they fit in my ears and it rarely falls out. It'll withstand even me running outside or on the treadmill. I also buy this consistently because of the quality of the product - the only reason I have to buy new ones is because I lose the old ones. There was one pair, however, that started to fall apart, but that's because I'm a clumsy person and I've had that pair for more than 2 years. I've never had a complaint about the audio quality.However, I did have to buy one recently, and the left ear just stopped working out of nowhere. This was less than a week after having it and after not having done anything to damage it. I've never had this issue before with this brand. I don't know if the color affects the quality (maybe different colors are made in different factory lines), but for what it's worth, the first one I bought was the green one, and the next two I bought were blue. I never had issues where I had to position the cord juust right with those three. But the one that suddenly stopped working was the purple one that I just bought.EDIT: It is now the third week I've had this pair of earphones and the right one completely stopped working as well. The phone registers that I have earphones plugged in (works with other earphones), but no sound is coming out of it. I submitted an email to the contact support team and am now awaiting a response.

title: Worked Once
body: I found this listed as a lightning deal on Amazon. It's sold as a product for children, but I bought it for myself because I have small ear canals. I wasn't expecting much, but I knew I wouldn't need them all that often. When they arrived, they looked like something you'd pull out of a cereal box. But the size was right, and they worked when I checked them. I also like the red color, because when all the other tech equipment is black, I can see the ear buds without a search.Last night I was listening to music online in the bedroom and my husband wanted to go to sleep. I figured I'd listen with the ear buds for another ten minutes before joining him.I was mistaken.When I plug the buds into my tower, the music stops. I unplug them and then the music isn't playing from the speaker system either! The heck? My spouse, who's an IT professional, said it seems like the act of plugging them in puts the music on pause. To get the music to play again, I had to refresh the page and reload my Amazon music. That done, I tried the ear buds again. Same thing happens. This doesn't occur with my other (uncomfortably larger) ear buds, just with these.How many parents are going to scold their children. "What do you mean they don't work anymore? We just gave them to you! I can't believe you already broke them!"Buying these things was the world's most false economy. Don't you do it.

title: THEY SWITCHED THE PRODUCT! DO NOT BUY!
body: Had a pair of these for years and wanted to get another. They were the best bang for your buck. Got the new pair and immediately knew something was off. They look like a knock off. The constriction is flimsier, not as precise. Keep in mind I've used my last pair heavily for years working construction and other rough jobs. These would never stand a chance. The sound quality was very poor, about what you get from a dollar store pair. No bass. The originals had great bass for an earbud. The mic quality is ok but sometimes shorts out (this is brand new) blasting the ears of the poor person on the other end of the line. The originals had a mic on the right side and the knock offs put it on the left. The idiot who made this trash didn't switch the wiring diagram so the sound is reversed. Try playing a game or watching a movie where an explosion happens on the right and you hear it on the left. If you pay more than $2 for these you got robbed. Now I got to find where I can get the original product because it is good and the reason why there are so many good reviews here.The picture of the ones without a mic look like the real deal. The picture of the ones with the mic are of the knock off. I have not tried the ones without a mic to confirm.Update: sound lost in one ear within 3 months.Edit: I can confirm the ones without mic are the high quality ones. The ones with the mic are a cheap knock off.

title: Update - But I still say Yuck.
body: Originally I said ...No bass response. Really; there is no bass response from these. I tried them with all three ear pads and fiddled around trying to get the best fit I could. I got the best low-end response with the largest pad which was basically no bass. Music sounded very thin and very mid-range heavy. Sort of like listening to the world through a toilet paper tube. Much worse sounding than my Apple earbuds.I loaded up my tone generator app and lowered the frequency until I heard the sound drop off. These Panasonic buds made it to 320 Hz. Same test with the Apple ear buds and they made it down to 100 Hz.These are either going back. If I get no refund I'll just toss them in the trash so they don't take up room in the house.----------------------------------But now after another attempt I find that the earbuds are almost nothing but bass. How is that? Well, as others have posted the sound of these changes radically once you push them into your ears and seat them as if you are putting earplugs in. Reach our opposite side hand over your head and pull your ear out and back, wiggle, push the earbud up into your ear canal and voila, you suddenly have bass.But you have almost nothing but bass. Thick, rich, muffled, weak highs. Oh, and yawn or wiggle your jaw around and the earbuds shift a bit and the sound changes.

title: I would like to return them because the sound is quite limited ...
body: I work as a professional orchestral musician. In purchasing earphones, affordability, convenience for travel/gym wear, and crisp sound are important to me. I would not purchase these earphones again and would return them if it was worth it to do so. Unfortunately, it is not given the cost to ship them to return and the small fee I would be getting back.I would like to return them because the sound is quite limited and I should have known better. There is a general "boominess" that covers everything as to make the entire listening experience not crisp. The low end (pitch-wise) is not clear and neither is the high end. Everything's sort of muddy.I ended up purchasing a pair of Rha s500i earphones from the Apple Store for $50 and I am very happy with them. The Rha are my favorite earphones which I've listened to but it won't be to everyone's liking; some may find it too dry and bass-less as much of the "boominess" is removed. For my purposes of listening to orchestral music, I really like just hearing the articulation of the bass with not too much echo of the bass (or of anything) sustained over the following music. If I did not purchase the Rha, I would use the current standard Apple earpods which have an in-store price of $30. I would rather spend more money on either of these other earphones than listen to these Panasonic earphones.

title: Not what it used to be
body: I've purchased 4 pairs of these without the mic in the past and have love them. So I thought I would get a pair with a mic so that I could listen to music and take calls without having to change headsets. I noticed right away that there were some differences in how they looked and not really in a good way. They looked cheaper than they did before but I felt like I can't be too picky with earbuds that cost so little, but the real problem came when I listened to music. I had to turn the music up higher than I usually do in order to hear it and the bass and sound quality were not nearly as good as my other pair. I've done a comparison of switching out my old pair with my new ones just to make sure I'm not imagining things and they are for sure lower quality sound. I'm very disappointed and will not be purchasing another pair in the future. I feel cheated because it seems like Panasonic is doing a bait-and-switch using the good reviews from a better product to sell an inferior product now.On a side note, one suggestion to Panasonic would be to make sure the mic is facing inward when the earbud is in the ear because the one I have faces out so I have to hold it in the right position while I talk or the other person just hears my surroundings instead of my voice. Again, these are cheap so I can understand, but it would make it a much nicer product.

title: Really Comfortable  ***UPDATED***  Garbage
body: These are actually some really comfortable ear buds. I usually prefer actual head phones, but these babies are really nice. They come with several different sized rubber inserts to customize the fit your ear size. I use the mid-sized ones. The sound quality is great. The first time I used these was on an international flight to watch movies and listen to music. The ones the airlines give you are absolute crap and I was so glad I had brought these with me. For the price, they can't be beat.**Update**I originally wrote the above review on October 30, 2013. I gave this product 5 stars at that time. It is April 16, 2014 now and I am going to have to change my review to one star. I use these headphones to listen to music and audio books. I keep the volume pretty low since I have pretty good hearing. Recently, the ear phones have started making a lot of hissing and crackling noises, like you would expert to hear form a set of headphones or speakers that had been blown because the volume was turned up too high. I am the only person that uses these and I have not done that. Nonetheless, the ear phones don't work properly anymore, which is very disappointing considering how lighlty used they are. I can't return them now, so I'll just have to throw them away. I would not recommend these if you want something to last.

title: Non-existent warranty / Lack of support / Low-quality Product
body: I've purchased these earbuds multiple times throughout this year because they've kept failing. I've purchased one this May and another this July, and when I got sick of repurchasing the item, I've attempted to contact Panasonic to have the item warrantied. When I called the Panasonic phone line that was listed on my order, I was informed that the wait time to reach a rep was around 20 minutes, and I might potentially be charged "$9.99" for the call. I hung up immediately and attempted the online support chat. Before the conversation even started, and before I said anything, I was told "this product has a 90 day warranty". Which leads me to believe Panasonic wants to discourage their users from attempting to warranty their items. I was told that in order to use the 90 day warranty, I will need to ship the item to one of their facilities, and I would need to pay for the shipping.Panasonic appears to go out of their way to provide short window for warranties, and even when you qualify for the warranty, discourages you even further by having you shell-out for shipping, which basically amounts to the cost of a new product.Needless to say, this will be the last time I purchase earbuds/earphones from Panasonic, and this will have bearing on my purchasing decisions in the future for all Panasonic products and services.

title: Go with something else. The rate is total wrong. Recommend S3 or S4 earphone
body: I don't know how this earphone has such a high rate. The sound quality is way worse than what I expected.I'm not the sound professional, I'm not picky sound freak. I expected the quality on Amazon review. I can say it is worse than earphone from Galaxy S3 which is selling for $5~$9 on Amazon.I lost earpad on Galaxy S3 earphone, so I bought this. Since it almost same price with S3 earphone, I expected this panansonic would have similar level of sound and material quality.*soundI'm not sure S3 earphones are genuine or not. But white earphone from Galaxy phone has way way better clear sound with more bass than this one. I'm not sure how I explain what I listen. As I said, I'm not professional. But I can say the sound is not clear to compare other earphone. It is noticable immediately if I compare with other earphones.*materialit has poor quality of cable to compare with $5~9 worth of samsung earphone. Cable looks like from late 90's or early 2000 if you remember thin cable that you can split half. The cable is really thin and looks like it is going to be broke if it gets some tension.Plus Galaxy S3 cable use gold plate. But this one doesn't.Seriously, go with samsung earphone. It has almost same price with mic, remote control, better sound quality.

title: NO BASS WHATSOEVER......THESE 5 STAR REVIEWERS ARE LYING TO US
body: These 5 star reviews had to be rigged by people who worked for Panasonic. THESE THINGS SOUND AWEFUL!!!; the worst earbuds I've ever used.....and I've used earbuds extensively for more than 20 years to listen to music while training at the gym. There is absolutely no bass range to these, so forget about listening to virtually any kind of music with them. Drums literally sound like tin can lids. This is the most inaccurately reviewed product I have ever bought from Amazon, nobody could legitimately give the sound of these over 2 stars and that is best case scenario. I sampled all different kinds of music too, from classical, to rock, to easy listening, to hard rock and thrash metal, and it all sounds terrible. I'm not holding these earbuds to any super high standard either, like comparing them with high-end full ear headphones.....I'm just comparing them to run of the mill earbuds you could buy at Walmart for $3-5 bucks. I can't use these, I hope amazon will let me return them. Who could enjoy their music when it sounds like a freakin cartoon soundtrack? My product does not appear to be defective either, like maybe I got a bad one.....these things by all appearances look to be built to sound like this...so if you buy them, they will almost certainly sound just like mine do. TERRIBLE PRODUCT!!!

title: Used to be great - now they're worse than garbage
body: I used to swear by this line of earbuds as a reasonably high-quality but affordable set of ear buds. Unfortunately I damaged my last pair, so I of course decided to reorder these based on my great prior experience. To my dismay the pair I received can only be described as worse than garbage. No, I'm not even sure that's a fair description because it doesn't do justice to the garbage headphones out there. Imagine that the music you love was first sent through a massive body of water for some good ol' distortion. After your music managed to cross the ocean, it was then transported childhood-style through a "tin can telephone" for refinement. Once/if the music had managed to survive that you get the privilege of listening to the songs you enjoyed being butchered.Want a better way to listen to music? Find a $0.99 bluetooth speaker from the depths of your local dumpster and give it a try. I'm sure it would surpass the quality of these headphones by a wide margin.With partial joking aside, I'm just disappointed that an affordable series of headphones has declined so significantly in quality from when I last purchased them. I purchased two of these, so the odds of it being just a defective unit are slim. Rather, I think the quality of the product has been compromised.

title: Much better sounding than they should for the price but quality control is lacking
body: These are really good sounding cheap earbuds. I use them for working out where I don't want to have to worry if they break or I lose them. I bought an extra pair to have on hand in case something does happen to them. They sound way better than they should be considering what you pay for them. The only thing I don't like about them is the way they position on your ear, they insert upside down in your ear and require you to wrap the cable around your ears. I prefer them to go straight down. The cable is also very flimsy and tangles too easily. Even so, these are really good sounding at an incredible price.Update Aug 2019I purchased a second r of these as a backup in case anything happened to the first ones. The first ones blew out but I used them a lot of times in the gym with hard use over three years.The second pair just sat in the unopened package on my shelf. I just opened the package of the new earbuds and the left earbud completely fell apart.I threw them in the garbage.I will have to look for another option for the gym. It seems the quality control has gone in the toilet over the three years I owned the first pair. I won't be buying them again.

title: Such high hopes, but alas...
body: I had such high hopes given the other reviews. This seemed like the perfect headphones at a great price. So good in fact I overlooked the fact that the first pair I bought didn't work and exchanged them for another pair. Amazon returns/exchanges are fast and easy (so five stars for Amazon). So I went zero good pairs out of two attempts. The issue I experienced was the same with both pairs I tried: the cord had to be perfectly situated or the sound got staticky, faded out or otherwise sounded like I was in an echo chamber or cave. When I moved my head or phone, and thus the cord, the sound would getter worse or better depending. I tried both pairs in multiple devices (iPhone and iPad) without success, and I did try other, known good headphones in those same devices and my known good headphones worked fine. Bottom line is that the problem was definitely with the quality of these Panasonics. Because of Amazon's return policy, if you don't mind taking a risk, I'd recommend getting these - who knows, maybe you'll be among the 1,000+ reviewers that had a great experience. Side note: I did order the headphones with the mic, so maybe those models are the problem.

title: Don't waste useful money on this unless you want a %50 chance of it being worth
body: Surprise surprise. It broke on the first day with me barely using it. The right audio has completely died out, along with the two others that I had previously bought (I bought three -silver, green, then black and they all eventually broke in a month span). I'm not even surprised, because I hadn't even thought about how horrible these earphones could have been due to past experience - I just needed earphones, and looky here - they broke...again for the 3rd time. This was a record for me - 2 hours after it arrived at the door. It didn't even have time to tangle! Oh..did I mention? If you enjoy stress, these earphones will possibly be the most stressful it can get in Amazon! For my past two earphones (same type) , the strings were incredibly thin, that if you just put them neatly in your pocket for about 5 minutes, they would come out with various knots everywhere. It may sound like a minor problem, but this is just an inconvienece. Another (around) 10 bucks down the drain. I'm not really one to add tons of details to some simple earphones but I sure wish somebody told me how bad these were - I would have bought some better earphones that actually last!

title: You get what you pay for
body: About $7 buys you earbuds that are moderately comfortable for about 20 minutes, then they feel like concrete in your ears. They have barely passable sound quality for most music types. Bass is miniscule. Treble is weak. I had to check my equalizer to make sure there wasn't a bandpass filter turned on somehow. Seriously, the earbuds included with my last Samsung smartphone are better than these.I used these for exactly two days before I got fed up and reordered my trusty, albeit $30 (gasp!), Sennheiser earbuds that I lost and that these were intended to replace. The Sennheisers sound infinitely better and I can wear them for about two hours before my ears start to notice they are in.If you listen to music for more than 20 minutes at a time, I highly suggest spending a little more and getting a quality earbud. If all you need earbuds for is listening to audio books for 15 minutes at a time, these will get the job done.I was hopeful after reading reviews that said these were great for $7. I am as frugal as the next guy, but these are not even worth $7 if you ask me. $6.50 for the Panasonic name, $0.50 for the earbuds.

title: Not even worth $2 - get a pair of MEE headphones instead.
body: The only positive thing I have to say for these headphone is that they are cheap and appear to be durably made.They are horribly uncomfortable when in your ear, and frequently fall out due to the shape - the ear piece is not square with the back side, but offset slightly. When you are wearing them, this has the effect of the cord constantly tugging the earphones out of your ear.I have tried many different ways to get the ear pieces to fit - including lifting the top of my ear while putting in the headphones, tilting them forward or backward - but not only are they uncomfortable, after about 15 minutes of use, they actually hurt my ears to wear by pressing uncomfortably on my ear canal because of their strange shape. The plastic is also very hard surrounding the ear piece and not adequately covered by the ear cover.The cord is very short, almost too short for me to use while I am running and holding my iPhone in my hand (with no tangles or wraps in the cord) - and I am not even 5' tall! The cord also tangles easily.Even if these are incredibly cheap, the experience are so terrible they are not worth it.

title: Damn So Much For Reviews
body: I got to say I always read reviews before I buy anything, But I was fooled by the reviews I read here about these Earphones! These have to be the worse Earphones I have ever heard. Yes they fit great in my ears But that's the end of it. There is no Bass what so ever, And no Volume either, These are so lifeless, I am not Big on a lot of Bass so these looked to be the earphones for me, I am on a fixed income and can't buy real high end phones However these seemed to fit what I was looking for. I own a few Panasonic items in my Home and love them all But I am so not happy with these Phones! I must say the pair that came with my MP3 player Blow these away! I just ordered a set of JVC Phones and I hope for the Best. If you are looking for a set of Earphones that are lifeless in sound in every way, Then these are for you! The Seller was wonderful But for the price of these I am not going to bother to return them, I think I will just give them to my 2 Year old Grand Daughter! Let me say one more time, Amazon is great! The Seller was also Great! And I will still buy here as always. Do yourself a favor Stay away from these earphones You will be sorry!

title: No Longer Reliable
body: I tend to buy a few of these, including the ones with the mic, to have as backups for myself and the kids for when the older ones go bad which is usually between 8-12 months. Anyhow, I opened up a new one of these and I noticed that the base that connects to your phone/MP3 players was thicker than usual. The second I connected it one of the headphones started to give out although slightly. I thought that was odd as it is usually a sign of the headphones going bad. Long story short one of the headphones went out after a week of use. Amazon was kind enough to refund me but then I opened up another pair and the base was again thicker. I bought them from different sellers so many it is a new Panasonic build for these? As soon as I connected the 2nd pair I got the same issue where one headphone gave out slightly. I now have to move it around so both headphones work. The sad part is that the same thing has happened to the Panasonic headphone with the mic. Those lasted me about a month before one headphone died out on me. I used to love these headphones but will no longer purchase them. It has been fun, Panasonic.

title: Beware as these are not able to be returned through Amazon if defective.
body: When I first opened these they fit very comfortable in my ears. When I tried listening to them the sound in the right ear kept shorting out. Went to Amazon to try to return them and learned I could not return them even though I had just purchased them a few days ago. Instead I was given the phone number to Panasonic customer support. I was disconnected twice from customer support while they tried to transfer me to the right person. I realize these headphones we’re only $8 but it’s the principle of it that they make you jump through all of these hoops from the manufacturer to return a defective product. Normally Amazon returns items quickly with little effort. Given all of the positive reviews of these headphones I am assuming I just got a defective pair.UPDATE: I called Panasonic to start a return. They told me I would have to ship the $8 earbuds to them first then they would fix them and send them back to me. Very disappointed as it would cost me at least that much to ship them in the first place. Not a fan of Panasonic right now and surely will never buy any Panasonic headphones again.

title: UPDATE - DO NOT Buy These !
body: UPDATE 12/30/15 - I have had these three months and the coating along the wires is falling off. The inner wires are exposed and I'm now afraid to use them. If I could return them at this point, I would. What a waste.I work out a newly-built World Gym. If anyone has been to a World, you'll know that they blast techo-pop music. If your work at at 6am like me, you likely also get a few senior citizens who go to the gym to socialize. They like to yell to one another other the music, amazingly always within close proximity to me for some reason. So I often find myself turning up my phone's media volume, to the point where Samsung is like 'Woah, you're gonna kill your ears!"I wore these Panasonic in-ear headphones to the gym today and purposely used an elliptical right next to the The Talkies, as I like to refer to them as. I barely heard a word, and my phone's media volume to low to medium at most. So for noise cancelling - 5 stars!These are very comfortable to wear in my ears, 5 stars again. The purple cord was true to the color shown - 5 stars!

title: Ruined my phone's headphone jack?
body: When I first got these they worked perfectly fine, albeit the plug had a habit of popping out easily. I originally attributed it to the plug not fitting in with my phone's clunky case, although I later found that that wasn't it.Fast forward a few months and music/sound constantly interrupts or won't play at all unless the plug is turned at the right angle. When I bought a new pair of headphones (same brand but different listing) they barely worked at all with my phone, despite working fine on any other device I've tried. The only exception to this is the laptop I've used frequently with these earbuds, as I've experienced some sound interruptions with the new pair as well.Finally, I've noticed that the plug on this pair has some noticeable scratches along the base, indicating some sort of damage from use.Overall, I suspect that these plug on these earbuds is defective and has damaged my devices' headphone jacks as a result. Judging by others reviews, these may indeed be counterfeit as I've never had issues with Panasonic earbuds before. Buy at your own risk.

title: Bad headphones: mic stopped working, screwy volume issue
body: I purchased these headphones for many uses: most importantly, to use on client calls (hence why I bought a pair with a mic), as well as to listen to music while running. These worked fine for a few weeks. Then sometimes on client calls the other person couldn't hear me, or the call would be covered by static. The mic has gotten worse and on my last couple of calls didn't work at all, so I was forced to crunch my shoulder to my ear while typing. Then, while listening to music or podcasts with these headphones, multiple times an hour the volume would inexplicably go to zero. At first I thought the internet was cutting out, but when I stopped and looked at my phone, the podcast would still be playing; and I finally realized it was the volume. I don't know what's wrong with them - they don't even have a volume control, no idea why they would cause my phone's volume to bottom out.All in all, a terrible purchase - bad for listening and for speaking. Wish I could get my money back but by the time I got frustrated enough to act it was beyond the return date.

title: Too much Treble, No Bass in mine
body: I don't usually rate so harsh on a product but these are epic fail There is no fidelity at all. I look at all the reviews about how good they sound and I have to come to the conclusion that some of the buds are built defectively in a production run. Mine are nothing but treble. And before anyone says they not plugged in correctly or EQ, let me state I mix audio and video all the time and know how to set up an mp3 player.They sound like they are out of phase, thus the treble comments by some reviewers. I went in and double checked eq settings of my Sandisk clip mp3 player, the only one that sounded tolerable is if I set the EQ to low frequencies boosted all the way and the other 4 bands decreased as far as they would go into negative territory. This is no way to set the EQ, that's when I decided it was a phase problem. Which they are cheap, not worth sending back but just so you know.I also checked impedance, these buds should be kicking but no go. The Sansa earbuds I was trying to upgrade from blow these out of the water, no comparison.

title: NOT FOR TINY EARS!!!!
body: Was soooo disappointed in these.... The audio was adequate, no complaints there, but the speaker stalk (or whatever it's called where the spongey part attaches to the bud itself) is shaped different from any other I've ever seen.The stem is shaped like a flattened oval, not even close to a circle. They were painful as soon as I put them into my tiny ears, and they were so painful I had to remove them within 5-10 minutes of firs wearing them. I tried to wear them a second time and gave up after a minute; they were just too painful.Unfortunately I put them away somewhere out of annoyance, and I can't find them again to take a picture of what I mean.For real, if your ears are so small that you use the tiniest sponge setting, only take a chance on these if you're willing to waste a few bucks. I just bought the Marley brand earbuds and prefer everything about them significantly. Yes, they're $15, so about double the price of these, but worth every penny compared to these Panasonic. They're better in sound quality AND tiny ear comfort!!

title: Do not buy these.
body: First, I read many positive reviews before ordering these and while there were a few negative reviews the + outweighed the -. Let me say that I'm not an audiophile and my hearing isn't what it used to be. I currently own a pair of Panasonic RPHC55 earbuds which are no longer available and they are great. I wanted something less bulky (the RPHC55s have a pre-amp inline) but didn't want to pay a lot of money. Although I've always been told you get what you pay for these seemed to be a good value for the money. I plugged them into my Galaxy Nexus phone and began listening to music and was immediately struck by how little bass there was and how tinny they sounded. Hoping that an equalizer would enhance my listening experience I tried 3 different equalizers on my phone with minimal improvement. Even the stock earbuds that came with my phone outperform these. Yes, they're just not good. Perhaps I got a lemon and other buyers got oranges? All I know is that I'm out $6 and thankfully not more. They'll probably be used very little and I'll just keep using my RPHC55 buds.

title: TERRIBLE washed-out sound, cheap, and pokey in the ears.
body: The sound quality is tinny, doesn't hold up to even the cheapest pair of earbuds in a gas station, and are not available for return or refund.I put these in for about 30 seconds and had to take them out. I listen to a little music during work hours, but I primarily listen to audio books at work. And the tinny washed-out sound these gave to Frank Muller's voice ruined a few minutes of a great story for me. They are sub-par quality, and for only $3 more per pair, I should have purchased 2 more sets of these: http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00UP992QE?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o07_s00I purchased 2 pairs of these to keep at work or in my home gym so I don't ruin the better ones I use for making calls, but they're such poor quality I feel bad even trying to give them away now.Also, the earbuds themselves have a few scratchy lines that when inserting them into my ears scratched the skin a little.PROS: NONE (well, maybe the color is strong enough to find them easily?)CONS: Everything

title: Worked for awhile
body: I originally bought this product back in August in purple and loved it. I loved it so much that when I got my new ipod Touch in red I ordered the red earbuds. I have very sensitive and small ears and this product is perfect for them. They do not hurt my ears at all.However after only a few weeks of use it started having problems playing the music. I noticed if the cord moved a certain way the music became distorted or stopped. This has just gotten progressively worse and now they are completely useless. I ordered them October 25th so I've only had them almost 2 months! and already they are useless! I had my dad an electrician look at them without taking them apart and he say it sounds like there is a short in the wire. That's great. Sounds like there has been since the beginning. So now I'm stuck with an paid for earbuds that don't work and I can't use. I want these particular earbuds because of the way they fit in my ear but now I don't trust that they will last or work. I mean I've had them less than 2 months and can't use them at all anymore.

title: BE CAREFUL BEFORE YOU DEICED TO BUY THEIR PRODUCTS. ONE PRODUCT HAS DIFFERENT QUALITIES
body: They have two different qualities from the same product. I bought one at the beginning that I was totally satisfied with the quality. Around a month later, I ordered another one for my brother. It turned out the new one has a really cheap quality and it doesn't work and have so many issues.My brother noticed that even the call answering button was on the left cord, usually it is on the right cord. So I checked the[[ASIN:B00E4LGU1Y Panasonic ErgoFit In-Ear Earbuds Headphones with Mic/Controller RP-TCM125-W (White)]] packaging and it turned out the packaging was different from the first one I got. Even the quality of the packaging was a cheap quality packaging. Also, same products have different barcode numbers and different model numbers. The cheap quality has an instruction paper that was in the packaging and most of it was written in Chinese. I'm totally disappointed since I felt that was clearly deception. I ordered a replacement for the one I got and I got another one with the same good quality. That's where I believe that they are cheating on customers.

title: These earbuds may slay dragons, but they are not very functional
body: These earbuds are very comfortable, stay in, and have really good sound. That's where the good news stops. There are several problems.(1) The jack doesn't stay reliably plugged into my tablet (HP Elite X12) or smartphone (LG V20), even just fidgeting a bit while sitting. It's pretty annoying to constantly have to push it back in, especially on my phone.(2) I can only adjust the volume on the attached device, not on the earbud wire on the mic section. The 'mic/volume' control does not work at all.(3) I can't use them when taking a call on my phone. The mike does not work at all when using them with my smartphone (LG V20). I can hear my caller's voice in the earbud, but they cannot hear me, even on the phone mic itself, unless I unplug these earphones. No amount of fiddling with the volume helps.These earbuds may slay dragons, but the ones around here are pretty tame. I generally leave them alone, as long as they leave me alone. I'd prefer earbuds that are more functional for their intended purpose.

title: None of them lasted, last one broke days after return window closed
body: With the first set, after less than 6 months, one of the channels has stopped working, and it is that way with all of the devices I have used it with, so it is definitely this product that is broken.The second set I got was faulty and didn't work so I returned it.The third set has failed after about 9 months in the same way as with the others - sound is only coming out of one channel. What rubbish. If I could find something better I'd never buy these again. If they have to be replaced every 6 to 9 months, that gets very expensive. I've got over the ear headphones that cost about $40 that I've had for a lot longer (years) that still work but I got these because they are more comfortable to use lying down.Yet another set is the worst yet. This one failed just days after the return window closed; no sound from one channel, the other so faint it can barely be heard with the volume turned all the way up. Since I haven't even used it part of the time I've had it, it hasn't even lasted a month, and amazon's attitude is get stuffed.

title: Should probably buy the warranty
body: These used to last me half a year - but now they last me a couple of months. The last pair went dead in the left bud and my current pair went dead on the right bud. I have gone through at least 5 of these in the past 2 and a half years. They are getting worse and worse I swear. I should buy the warranty but I always forget. I think I should just invest in better earbuds. And not those airpods crap - there is no way people can not not lose them.Is it me? Are earbuds only supposed to last a short amount of time? I used to swing my buds around like a lasso (a bad habit) but I stopped a while ago because they kept causing them to break. Now, I'm treat my earbuds so well, yet they still die on me. Are all the affordable earbuds just total garbage? I heard that Beats by Dr. Dre only cost $14 to make, yet are sold for like $200. So, earbuds must cost $0.50 to manufacture - so I feel so cheated when I spend more than $10 on a pair. If anyone has any affordable, quality earbud recommendations, please feel free to message me.

title: Cord Tangles and Has Right Angle Plug
body: Cons:1. The cord is not silicone wire. It tangles easily.2. Right angle mini-plug puts excess stress on the wire. I expect it to fail. With straight mini plugs, I can install a piece of shrink tubing over the plug to provide increased stress relief where the wire goes into the plug. Can't fit shrink tubing onto a right angle plug.3. Size is a bit too large for comfortable sleeping. I use earbuds specifically to help me get to sleep by putting a droning podcast on at very low volume. Out like a light in a few minutes. The earbuds also act like earmuffs to mute room noise. These Panasonic buds are just a bit too long and are uncomfortable in the ear that's against the pillow.4. Audio quality is so-so but adequate for voice.5. Expensive for what they are. Can find four sets for less $$ elsewhere.Pros:1. The buds are angled such that you can see in daylight and feel in the dark which one is left and which is right.After they break, I won't be buying them again.

title: Meh.
body: Here's an honest review. The best thing about this earphone are the color options and the price. These are cheaply built, low quality sound earphones. The earbud color looks cute (I got the green one) but it looks cheap.It's hard to fish out for pros other than that. In terms of the sounds, there is virtually no sound stage whatsoever and the bass is very weak. If you're looking for earphones that will provide you with fair-good quality sounds, this is certainly not the earphone for you.The earpiece component itself is very weirdly structured. The silicon cover fits into an oval-shaped sound output which makes it extremely difficult when you're trying to put the silicon piece back on. And because of the oval design of the earpiece, they don't fit my ears very well and keeps falling out of my ears, even with the largest silicon cover. I find that very annoying.If you're looking for a better earphone, I'd recommend Skullcandy with flat cords. They've lasted me for a few years.

title: mediocre- don't bother wasting your money
body: First a frame of reference. I currently own Klipsh S4 and Etymotic ER6 earbuds. Also have owned the Bose earbuds and with respect to headphones, own Grado 125, Sony MDR VR6 and Sennheiser active cancellation headphones. Finally, I have an assorted collection of ipod/nano, Sansa Sandisk, Android and Blackberry earbuds. Had purchased the Panasonic buds for outdoor activities (biking, running, beach) and was wowed by the price and reviews. Having tried the earbuds, my conclusion (and it's not my first rodeo) is the the Panasonic buds are really bad....the sound quality is like that of a bad AM radio. For the money I think they are overpriced...I've heard better sound from the $2 buds given out on airlines. I'm stunned that these buds get good reviews. Maybe the majority of folks don't know what decent buds sound like...if so, great for them that they are happy. I cannot see any reason to use the buds...I play rock music but don't even see them being of reasonable quality for audio books. Don't waste your money..

title: No Bueno!
body: I have the Bose Sound Ultra for Android and they sound amazing to me. BUT, the cord keeps fraying at the junction of the plug that goes into my phone. I just had to order my 9th pair through Best Buy (who is no longer going to carry them for Android...only IOS). wanted was an economical pair to have in my backpack for "emergency". I ordered three different ones to see what sounded good to use until I find a permanent WIRED earphone with a tough cord made to survive the life of New York City with delicious sound. These things ... THESE THINGS.... they will be sent back tomorrow. They are HORRIBLE. I wasn't expecting to be wowed for the price. But the reviews made me think I might get somewhat of a "wow". Seriously, I cannot even believe I allowed myself to have hope. I can only hope Robert Smith of The Cure will forgive me for using his music to test these made for a six year old. There you have it. I wouldn't accept these things to listen to a voice read a book to me.

title: Great Sound Quality, But Extremely Subpar Construction
body: I bought these headphones in August and the left ear bud is already completely broken. No sound is emitting out of the headphone. The right side is still working but without the left, these are worthless. The sound was actually fantastic while they worked, but for such a low life span, I couldn't possibly rate these higher than 1 star. Poor heartiness is their key issue. I have only used them at work (I sit in a cube) under normal wear. I'm a bit disappointed considering I was actually pretty excited to find a pair of earbuds that actual fit snugly in my ears and had great quality sound. They are also somewhat noise canceling as well which is nice when you are trying to get work done in a noisy office environment.Pros: Great sound quality for earbuds. Earbud "rubbers" will most likely fit any ear and are very comfortable.Cons: Very fragile. Only last a few months under normal wear. No visible signs of damage, yet do not work.(Signs of weak internal construction)

title: Waste of money - they stopped working
body: First of all, I was shipped the wrong ones. So I had to return those and get new ones. They sent me the ones without the mic even though I ordered ones with the mic. I am mad because these stopped working within a month. They never leave my bedside table and I don't use them all that often. The right side (side with the mic) is what stopped working. It's not my phone because I use other headphones and an aux cord in my car. I don't have an iPhone so that weird dongle thing isn't an issue either. Not sure how they broke but whatever. Waste of money - don't buy.Otherwise, they were good. They are a good fit for my ears, sound quality wasn't amazing but it was pretty good. They had a nice long cord, although cheap-feeling. But I didn't care since I only ever used them while in my bed. I can't speak to the quality of the mic, since I never had a chance to use it. The pause button worked (and still does even though that bud is broken).Overall, total waste of money.

title: Quality is not great. Broke in 5 days.
body: These earphones started to fall apart in 5 days. The cord broke and ripped so the wires are exposed. I dont expect much from $15 ish earphones but I do expect it to hold up longer than 5 days. I also dont think $15 earphones should feel like this quality. I didnt treat it with poor handling. I simply used them, wrapped them up and placed it in my pocket after each use, pulled then out and plugged them in. It still broke.As for the quality, in my hands, the earphones feel like it was picked up from the dollar store, and not in a good way. I'm pretty bummed about this.The noise cancellation isn't great either. If the any part of the cord gets touched slightly, it will be very loud and can hear it through the ear piece/phone. I've also experienced some static when it was plugged in, something I never got with a corded earphone.The sound quality is not that great and I'm not an audiophile. I've heard worse, but these are pretty close.

title: Cord developed a short already
body: Okay, sure, it's cheap to buy this. And you may have different results than I did, but I had two problems with it.First, the cord developed a short within the first 40 days of ownership. I use it several times a week, and I'm using it while trail running, so the unit gets bounced around a bit. Still… 40 days? I've had other headphones I use while trail running that have lasted two or three years. I personally mind if the sound cuts out occasionally, so I had to downgrade this one considerably for value. A $50 headset that lasts two years is actually cheaper than a $10 headset that lasts less than two months.Second, I've never been able to get a solid fit with the earpiece design. I've tried different sizes of the earbuds, but not been able to get them to stay in place, unless I physically hold them with my hands into my ear canals. It's not easy to run a trail while doing that, so, in short, I don't think I'll ever be buying these again.

title: Old version was Super durable, new version is awful.
body: EDIT: I got 3 more pairs because I liked them but both of the new ones are pretty awful right out of the package! The sound cuts in and out when the cord is moved; There are scratchy sounds, and there is a lot of cable noise carried to your ears when the cords rub against something like your shirt collar. I see others here have noticed the same thing.OLD REVIEW:I have 2 pairs of these that I use interchangeably on a daily basis. I am very hard on earbuds: the cable catches on things twice a day or more and yanks them from my ears. They have survived for over a year with this treatment without any change in sound. No other earbuds I have used have lasted anywhere near this long without damage to sound from one or both.If you are using under a helmet with earflaps (for winter use) these can be a little sore for the ears, I can't use them that way for more than 15 minutes or so, but this is true for most earbuds under helmet ear-flaps.

title: Tinny sound
body: I bought these headphones based on other reviews that the sound quality was decent, but when I tried listening to a few different movies and TV shows with them, the sound was very tinny and had practically no bass. They are not advertised to have any kind of bass boost, so I wasn't expecting great bass, but there's practically no bass at all. With these headphones having such a tinny sound quality, I could hear every outside sound around me no matter how quiet the sound was, even with the movie volume cranked up to help block the outside sounds. Granted, they are not advertised to have any noise cancelling/blocking features, but in-ear headphones in general can usually block a small amount of external noise due to the design. With the tinny sound quality and no noise reduction even compared to cheaper in-ear units, I would not purchase these units. I have occasionally found dollar-store headphones with better quality than these. Take your money elsewhere.

title: Uncomfortable and low quality
body: I shopped around for ear buds and after reading lots of reviews and looking at prices I had to give these a try...wasted of money :( The first time I wore these was to the gym and within 30min they already got sore and that was with the smallest ear piece. I'm a fairly small girl, but I wouldnt say I have abnormally small ears, the hard part was just too big. Even though they hurt I didnt return them cause I figured I'd keep them until I found some I liked more, plus they only really start hurting around the hr mark and I dont last that long at the gym anyways :) However, they died before I could even order new ones. About the third time I used them they started cutting out which is lame. I've now used them maybe 10 times and I constantly have to be holding the plug at an exact angle to make them work...very annoying. I wouldn't buy these again, even for the $5 price, waste of money, but even more a waste of time cause now I have to re-research.

title: Product arrived defective, no return allowed
body: I've purchased these headphones three times, always have solid sound quality and fit comfortably in my ears. However, was very disappointed this product arrived damaged in the packaging (plastic wire covering defective, leaving part of the wires exposed). Would have just filed a return but received an error saying this product cannot be returned. Super frustrating that I'm now stuck with a defective product and no way to get my money back.*****Update, I checked further and sent a "get product support" message to see if there's an alternative way to get the product returned. Filled out a quick message, the next page simply includes the manufacturing contact info (1-800 support number) and a link to Panasonic's own website. Amazon's own link for "more assistance" at the bottom of the page is broken and just shows some code for a relative link, totally unusable. Just know if you get a defective item you're stuck!

title: Okay for the price....
body: Despite reading so many great reviews (raving reviews that had been the deciding factor for purchasing these), I ordered these headphones with average expectations - after all, they are being sold for quite an inexpensive price.Unfortunately, they left me disappointed. They might be a good fit for someone else, just not for me. Here's why....Pros:1. OK for watching videos online or on YouTube, or other non-musical activities. Clear sound, no static, and no unwanted echoing.2. They do a good job of blocking out external noise when listening to music, and when no music is playing while wearing them they still block out about at least half of the background noise.3. Probably ok for someone who listens to their music at a generally quieter level than most, those who do not mind losing much of a song's typical bass quality, and/or those with sensitive ears that require lower or less-intense audio and/or volume levels. Parents who would love their kids to have a good pair of volume-limiting headphones may appreciate these greatly. (See # 1 on my list of "Cons" below for why I am saying so....)4. The fit is a little different than some other ear buds on the market. This can be a pro or con depending on the person using them, but for me, they were comfortable to wear for decent periods of time without any ear discomfort. (Comfortably physically, not musically.) I wore them for about 2-3 hours at a time during a few online webinars.Cons:1. Loss of music quality and volume. Seriously... I would use the headphones that came with my iPod instead of ever using these if it came down to that. I say this because I found the volume maximum to be about the same, if not quieter, than even some of those intentionally lowered-volume headphones on the market today (the kind of headphones I'm referring to are the buds/headphones that set the maximum volume lower than the standard buds/headphones on the market in an attempt to proactively keep kids from going deaf when they decide to blast the volume on their iPods and mp3s). The only problem with that being, for someone like me who has great hearing and a love of music, the songs sound like they are at a partial volume despite both my iPod and laptop being at their respective maximum volume levels. I typically will never turn any of my devices up that high, but after receiving these and giving them a test-run, I was trying to see if raising the volume level on my devices would resolve the lack of satisfaction, excessive lack of bass, and the lack of volume in the music coming through these headphones. This leads me to the next con...2. The fit. Thinking that maybe I was using the wrong attachment size for my ears and that perhaps it was affecting the quality of the music or muffling the music somehow, I tried every single size of the attachments that had been provided. Despite the sizing not affecting the quality or volume level at all, I also found that it was difficult to figure out which way these are supposed to sit in the ear. I positioned them in the way my other in-ear headphones would sit, yet these still seemed to be 'off.' Perhaps it is because of the uniquely angled design? After playing with it for a bit, I found a comfortable position that allowed the noise-cancelling to work its magic; however the volume levels, while still on the quieter side and seemed to vary depending on the song that was playing. This caused me to keep readjusting them, almost as if they had somehow moved out of place or something. I have not had this problem with any other in-ear headphones I've purchased before. I would have concluded that these headphones not being positioned correctly was the cause for the volume/sound quality issues I was experiencing when using these, however after a couple hours of trying every position and depth humanly possible with these, I figure it's mainly the output I'm having an issue with, rather than their fit.Conclusion:I will probably keep these stashed away as back-up in case I lose whatever pair I am currently using to listen to my music with, or perhaps only use them for webinars and the like, but these just weren't a good fit for me. For the price, maybe these aren't so bad (especially if these are the qualities you are looking for in a pair of headphones), but I have never spent more than $15 on headphones or ear buds in my life and these didn't compare very well to the many others I have tried. I even thought for a moment that maybe I'd just lost some hearing or gone a little deaf over time when I realized how limited these headphones sounded to me, but my friend let me borrow her JVC gumy headphones to test my ears and the audio/volume from her headphones turned out to be fine, even at my device's lower volume levels. The JVC gumy ear buds she lent me were just like the gumys I used to buy regularly every six months or so prior to purchasing these Panasonic headphones. The JVC gumys sound amazing and don't really sacrifice any bass in spite of their ridiculously affordable price, but after the prop 65 warning that they are required to share with consumers had been posted, I chose to move on to healthier audio pastures. I had truly hoped these Panasonic headphones would be just that - but they aren't. Just ordered the next pair of headphones I'll be trying out from Amazon this morning and we'll see how they measure up.

title: HORRIBLE, disappointing sound and fit. Can't believe thousands of people have given these good reviews!!
body: I read review after review of these earbuds, just to be sure I was getting something as good as the ones I've had for 2 years that were great, though did not fit so well. The ones that did not fit well had bigger speakers, plain and simple, and though they were in-ear buds, were just slightly too large for me, but they SOUNDED GREAT! However, after reading reviews of these Panasonics, (RPTCM125V) I bought them, and they are HORRIBLE earbuds. I read several reviews that said they had to be well seated in the ear. I tried, tried again, and again, swiveled them this way and that, and they simply don't provide enough range to their sound unless I hold pressure on them while listening, and even then, not as much depth as my previous pair. That is not a reasonable expectation.So what I get from my old ones, which are about 12mm diameter and ROUND, is FULL range of sounds, very nice base, very nice mids, excellent highs, and little external noise - just enough to hear a horn or someone yelling. With these Panasonics I can hear everything that happens around me, and can barely hear my music, and there is ZERO bass to these - listening to Milt Hinton (Jazz bassist) and I can't hear... literally can't hear his base. Put my old ones in, sounds great. I have more than one pair of these Panasonics, both the same, so it's not just a bum pair. So yes, some people said you have to really get these to fit right, but if you don't, these are terrible. I never could. Tried all three rubber nibs that came with them. none would fit correctly. Very disappointing, except that I paid $7.00 ish for them, so don't mind keeping them as emergency buds. Bummer my old ones have now lost one side.Now, I'm a particular person, and I am somewhat of an electronics person, audiophile etc. So, while I don't re-hash all the specs on things, the claims of others that they sound great are likely because they've never had a truly good pair. I had to go up three notches on my iPhone volume to hear well with the Panasonics. That one factor alone tells me that these are just too little speaker for decent sound. If I can't get enough volume, then there is not enough speaker.There it is.. my 2 star review. 2 stars because they're cheap and do work.Don't buy'em if you are REALLY looking for good sound. If you generally don't care, buy two. They're cheap.Cheers!

title: Great Audio  Fidelity (But) Easily The Most Painful Earbuds I've Ever Used
body: I bought these on the strength of positive reviews for the sound quality, and these are indeed excellent-sounding earbuds. The problem is that in order to get that deep, rich audio - particularly bass response - these have to be really well-embedded in your ear, and these are so painful to wear that it's borderline intolerable. And that's surprising, because Panasonic is a company I generally trust to produce great stuff.These are all hard edges and unyielding, bone-crunching solidity where there should be soft-as-a-pillow comfort, and a veritable 3D geometry puzzle of angles and tangents and things evocative of the proverbial dog's hind leg where a simple straight line would be fine.They're hard to get far enough into your ear because of that big disc-shaped thing in the middle of the horizontal axis; they hurt like hell all the way in because the pads that are supposed to provide cushion between the plastic and your living tissue are both small and thin; and they're hard to keep in your ear once they're there (assuming you've chosen to grit your teeth and take the pain for the sake of music,) because the business end that houses the actual speaker is too small in diameter and too short in extent to get any useful friction - particularly when that big disc thing is snugged up against your dorsal metamoglopsolesis or whatever it's called, which is pushing it in the opposite direction, namely: out.Lest you assume I have abnormal ears, I've used dozens of different earbuds and earphones and roughly half of them feel better in your ear than nothing at all. 'Don't know about Panasonic's other models, but this one is just a mechanical design gaffe.Believe it or not, some of the best-sounding - and most comfortable - earbuds I've come across are the pairs that come with the Roku streaming TV units. I hadn't noticed until I heard some good, punchy rock in some movie soundtrack or other, was duly shocked at the audio quality, and tried them with my MP3 player. I like them so much that I went to the Roku site and bought a three-pack of them. [And no, I'm in no way affiliated with Roku and nobody is paying me to say this. 'Just the facts, ma'am.]So unless you've got a high tolerance for pain, stay away from these. Yes, they're dirt cheap, but... they hurt like hell.

title: Not as good as AKG earbuds of same price level
body: I used to listen to all my music with these panasonic earbuds, and never felt I needed anything else. I've lost them and replaced them several times over the years and was always happy with them. However, lately I've been listening with a pair of AKG earbuds I found laying around. They are the same price as these Panasonics, however their treble is way brighter and their bass actually exists noticeably. More importantly, the AKG earbuds sound more crystal clear compared to these, which sound dull and blurbled by comparison. I compared the two side by side from a few of my favorite spotify songs and a couple of scenes from recently streamed shows/movies (dialogue scenes, action scenes with explosions, etc), and found the AKG noticeable better in every scenario. The tangleless braided cable on the AKG is superior to these panasonics as well. I don't know what happened to Panasonic, but they are just not as good as I remember... very disappointed with it.Link for AKG earbuds: https://www.amazon.com/Samsung-Stereo-Headphones-Microphone-Galaxy/dp/B07PTXSHWR/ref=asc_df_B07PTXSHWR/?tag=hyprod-20&linkCode=df0&hvadid=241977007607&hvpos=&hvnetw=g&hvrand=14715958201647687112&hvpone=&hvptwo=&hvqmt=&hvdev=c&hvdvcmdl=&hvlocint=&hvlocphy=9011931&hvtargid=pla-682219160298&psc=1The only useful aspect of these earbuds is the noise cancelling aspect of the plug if one of the 3 sizes fits comfortably enough in your ear, which I find they do. Would recommend just cutting the wire and using the earbuds as effective ear plugs to block out the noise when sleeping or working in a noisy environment. I also just noticed It seems they also added a sharp surface on the outside edge of the earbud that pushes against your outer ear, and will leave an imprint on your outer ear if you're laying down with them, so maybe don't use them for sleeping after all.They somehow managed to ruin an already great ergonomic design by adding this sharp edge to the outside brim. The only explanation I can think of is they moved their manufacturing factory plants to China.

title: sound quality is poor without amplification
body: This is a slightly upspec version of their 120. The only difference is that this version has a mic and a pause and playback button, but the sound quality is identical.That is to say, sound quality is poor without amplification. Add a nice little headphone amp such as the popular Fiio E6, and SQ improves markedly: which becomes acceptable, rather than awful. As I stated in the review of the 120, the 125 has absolutely anemic bass: very weak with very little low end extension. Treble response is sharply rolled off.Add an amp, and both the low and hi end improve substantially. Bass is much stronger, but becomes a little boomy. The high end can actually be heard, but extension is still pretty bad, and the bass bleed and prominence are probably overwhelming to boot.This is a fine pair of earbuds as a low cost option, if your budget is severely limited.If you want better sound quality, check out philips 3580/3590 instead. Those sound like a very downscale version of grado's.I'm always surprised by the reviews these low budget earbuds receive. I can't see these being rated higher than a 1 or 2 star product on sound quality alone. Perhaps they deserve an extra star if you're feeling generous because of the exceptionally low price, but they shouldn't be rated highly just because they're cheap IMO. Every product should be rated on the basis of quality, rather than strictly on the basis of cost.Then again, since most amazon reviewer is willing to give most every pair of earbuds a positive review, it's likely that whoever is buying these will like them regardless.You would think that in 2016, that electronics products would have improved to the point where high fidelity sound would be both ubiquitous and cheap. It's astonishing how short of the mark most audio electronics fall to this day. Sound quality hasn't improved much over cassette tape technology at the low end of consumer electronics in the 21st century.

title: LOVE experience quality; HATE durability quality
body: I bought this product, thinking that it would last. I have thought wrong. This product broke in a bizarre and insignificant place. That place would be the metal bit that plugs into the jack of a device. PICTURE SHOWN BELOW FOR INITIAL 2016 PURCHASE.EDIT FOR SECOND PURCHASE May 10, 2017: Repurchased as soon as first pair broke. Intention of buying again is for the comfortability, quality OF SOUND, and ergonomic features (includes the "ErgoFit" ear part, cord thinness, and ninety degree angle jack). Nearing the end of May 2018, the left ear stopped working. This is the common problem of a broken internal wire, where you can hear the sound again if bent at a certain degree.EDIT FOR THIRD PURCHASE July 1, 2018: Repurchased AGAIN for the third, but maybe not the last time. I really enjoy these earbuds, my favorite In-ear of all time. Look forward for more updates that might occur once this new pair breaks.Edit purchase #4: ive bought my last pair in black that has finally gave its last chirp (in the left ear). So i have decided again to consume this product and once again give money for a “metallic red” pair this time.Im just so used to the hje120s that i stick with it, though was contemplating with throwing my money at some other big brand. These are so common in my everyday use that once the left side broke, i just had to quickly make the decision to fall for these budget buds again… which of course had let me down.My brand new pair is noticeably defective enough to a non-audiophile’s ear. The sound is unbalanced, quieter in the left hand side. It sounds like someone in the center, but is facing to the right. It is unbearable to me and hinders my focus and performance. Planning for replacement. 😔Conclusion: these are amazing for budget (back when they were $6-$7 a pop), but after numerous purchases, i am now looking for higher quality. Oh, and also a better microphone. those look and sound horrid. ✌️

title: Not suited for outside light use
body: I've had the earbuds for about 2 months now, and although the quality of the sound is pretty decent (I would say on par with Skullcandy earbuds of the same price), there are too many negative factors that make it not worth getting.Perhaps I should differentiate the casual laptop user with the pedestrian music listener and gym-goer. The earbuds were really a 1-star to me because I walk around a lot with my android and I used these for working out. Because of its short cord length, you have to hunch over and bend your back/neck if you run it through your shirt.
