But for my tastes...listening to jazz (Diana Krall type stuff)...I found the Panasonics more to my liking, but both are at least 50 times better than the ones I had, and these were only a few bucks more.I can't figure out how you can buy this kind of quality for such an incredibly cheap price! Gotta love Amazon!~Lastly, I visited JLabs website and saw that these earbuds were their "low line" buds. If their lower line buds sound as good as they do, I think it would be interesting to spend $20-30 to see what their higher-end models sound like.With either brand, you can't go wrong!

title: Pretty impressive
body: I was prepared to be underwhelmed with these, but I'm pretty impressed. The sound quality is very good. These are better than my headphones from Sony. The bass has depth & the highs are clean. I listen to Pitbull station on Pandora when I work out & the songs sounded really good. I've used my Sony headphones with my phone for as long as I've had my phone & assumed the sound was as good as it was going to get due to my phone. Not the case at all. "Don't Let Me Down", by Chainsmokes "Don't Stop the Party", by Pitbull never sounded so good! The earpieces are made from hard plastic & The piece that goes into the ear is covered in a silicon type material which is pretty comfortable in that they slip in & out pretty well but don't slip out on their own. The headphones come with a second set of silicon covers which is nice.Downside:The earpieces are labeled "R" & "L" but because the letters are very, VERY small, without my glasses on, it's really hard to tell which is which & in the gym, the lights aren't the brightest so it will be very difficult to tell which earpiece goes in. The way I'll be able to tell is how they fit. I purposefully put them in the wrong ears to see if I could notice the difference & I absolutely could. My plan is to put a spot of bright nail polish on the "R" earpiece so I'll know which goes where. The other downside is the earpieces must be placed in MY ears a certain way of they make my ears sore after wearing them for longer than 30 minutes. My typical session at the gym is 1.5 hours. As long as they are situated properly, all is well. I know pretty quickly when they aren't sitting right & they are easy to move around manually but don't move around on their own so that is good. The last bit that I don't love is the L shape of the pug-in end of the cord. If I don't push hard enough, my protective phone case impedes a proper connection & the music plays from my phone & not through my headphones. In the future, I'd love to see a straight jack end or a longer piece so it can connect without worry of the phone case thickness.Overall take:For the price, these provide phenomenal sound quality. The ear pieces can be somewhat finicky, but that could also be my ears & not the overall take from the masses. The silicon covers that slide into the ear are smooth & slide in easily but don't slip out. The cord is long enough for the headphones to be in your ears & your phone can sit on the piece of cardio equipment without feeling too short or falling off due to too little slack. Would I buy these again? absolutely. For those who are looking for a color other than black, these are perfect. The headphones come in a lot of colors. Overall, you are getting a great product & for the price, so far, these just can't be beat.

title: Your next best BUDdies
body: Tired of having earbuds quit working on you after a year or two, no matter how much you spent and how many good reviews there were?Hate it when the connection turns bad because of that creasing where the reinforced jack meets the cord?THESE will solve everything!!These reliable BUDdies (pun intended) have blocked out the noises of chatterboxes at the library, solicitors along the path to class, and meatheads whose grunts match the weight of their egos. All while filling my ears with tender (or ratchet) sounds of music like the vocal equivalents of butter (or flamin hot cheetos con limon) to my tastebuds.Ok but actually, in all seriousness...I bought these 4 years ago, and here's what really amazes me--they still work as perfectly as they did when I first bought them (FOUR!! years ago).Used them daily for about 2 years (walking to class, while studying, etc.). During the latter 2 years, I've used them a few times a week for running/going to the gym. And they still. work. perfectly. AMAZING isn't it?! Especially for the price!Don't get me wrong, they've definitely seen a good amount of abuse. The times I've forgotten to remove them from my ears when I got up and subsequently dragged my laptop while ripping them out of the plug... when I've shoved them into my backpack with who knows what snack crumbs are hidden in there.. or when I toss them into my purse and Purell leaks everywhere--literally everywhere--and into all the crevices (but at least that way it gets disinfected)... IT STILL WORKS! And those little ear pads have never fallen off on me (except for that time I was blindly walking around and simultaneously stepped upon the tips and pulled up the cords, forcing the detachment... yeah, I'm pretty clumsy).When I purchased these, I was fully convinced I would have to eventually replace it, since I had gone through about 3 other noise-canceling earbuds prior. But I haven't had to just yet, and I am more thankful than ever for these buddies. They've blocked noise, delivered high-quality sound, and lasted me for so long that I'm actually getting sentimental just thinking about the good times we've had together. I would be devastated if they ever quit on me. In the realm of earbuds, these will be your BFFLs.Here's an extra tip: keep your earbuds (relatively) untangled with the simple solution of a binder clip! Just wrap the cord around your fingers, then clip together with binder clip! Not perfect and occasionally I'll still manage to somehow get them tangled because I'm me, but it's definitely saved me a ton of frustration compared to not having one at all. Also much quicker than wrapping around something like those stick-shaped holders.

title: Crazy good, and I've tried most of the best brands and types.
body: I have many different types of headphones and earbuds (and of differing styles: noise canceling, in ear, sealing in ear (this type here). I have models of earbuds for 30 up to 150 bucks. These are stunningly good. I was going to add "for the money" but, "for the money" they are ridiculously good. I don't know what the other companies and brands are spending to produce theirs but if you can make and sell at a profit earphones that sound this good for 7 bucks, most of the major companies are doing something wrong (or super right in terms of profits and brand name). These are terrific. The cord is kind of cheap and nothing to write home about, but no worse than that which comes with iPods. But the sound is vastly better.No mic. These are not for smart phone use for making calls, just for listening to music and movies and such. They come with three sizes of the ear things. A tight but comfortable fit in your ear canal is essential for this type of bud to sound right. The large ones work best for me on this model.EXCELLENT base response. However, that tends to be true of all models like these IF you can get the ear sealing fit right and also comfortable. Otherwise, balance and tonal quality is warmish but not mushy. I'm stunned and impressed. As someone else (younger than me said here, in the vernacular of the day, these are SICK. As in, SICK = AMAZING for 7 bucks. They beat anything I've tried ever under 80 bucks. And my current fans at 80 bucks do not sound better--well maybe a tad ,but since I cannot do a blind listening test, I'm biased by knowing which were the expensive ones. If you want this type of model of ear buds, these are terrific. Just watch the volume levels since these seal your ear canal. The potential for damage to your hearing is greater than headphones that sit over or one your ears, or the type of buds that fit more loosely in your outer ear canal (my all-time favorites of those types, by the way, happen to be a kind of Panasonic you just cannot get anymore. They came with these plastic mp3 players they used to sell. Best sounding, open ear ear buds in history. Really. I bought a bunch of the cheapo mp3 players when they were going out of existence just to get enough of those open ear ones to last me many many years (open ear ones are better for running, biking, working out, etc., where you need to also be able to hear what's going on around you.) Anyway, panasonic likely understands something about earbuds other companies don't or don't want to act on (like a more powerful magnet maybe for the drivers?)Get these. They are great. And if you don't like them or can't get best sealing fit, you are out 7 bucks. To trips to starbucks.

title: Not "Tinny" as One-Star Reviewers Claim
body: After using these earphones for an average of 3 hours every day, they finally broke yesterday (i.e. one side would cut out unless I manipulated the headphone jack and held the cord in a particular way). For 11 months of constant use, wrapping up the cord, shoving my iPod & headphones in various pockets and backpacks, etc. I feel this is reasonable based on the price. I will buy another pair.I can confirm from use that the noise canceling aspect of the way they fit is great. Also I now prefer the feel/fit of these over the Apple earbuds--the old Apple earbuds. I tried the new Apple earbuds and I must say that they are freakish and I hate them.~~~~~~~ Original Review from Aug 9 2012: ~~~~~~~I bought these because they are cheap and had good reviews. Still I always read the 1-star reviews because they are good at identifying the flaws. I was a bit apprehensive upon buying these because so many people said the bass was no good and as a result they were not good for music. I just got my pair and I can say that this is not true. The sound quality is great. Not just great for the price but great period. I cannot tell any difference in sound quality between these and the Apple earbuds which I am replacing. Other important info: unlike other earbuds, all of which could be called "in ear," these earbuds are actually "in ear canal" buds. You see that little jelly/rubbery part on the end of the earbud in the photo? It squeezes in your ear canal. It is definitely a different feel than a regular earbud, and I have to say I prefer the feel of apple or other similar earbuds which don't penetrate me so much! That said, the design is good so this is not uncomfortable, and I am still giving it 5-stars because I suspect that this gives these earbuds a better noise-canceling effect (because they are like earplugs and earphones in one). Since I use mine on my moped this is a plus--but even for regular use on the street, bus, mall, etc. I imagine this is good for everyone. Also: they seem to get louder than the Apple headphones. I don't know if this is because they actually play louder or another result of the in-ear positioning but this is another plus for me because sometimes I need it loud! to overcome external noise. For the price these are amazing and I already added another pair to my cart to purchase for a backup. The saying "you get what you pay for" is not the case here: these earbuds deliver $20-$25 quality at a fraction of the price. Now the only thing left is to test their durability. If they don't hold up you can bet I will be updating this review. I hope this info is useful to you in making your buying decision.

title: Can't beat these for the money!
body: My beloved Koss KE29 earbuds died after two years of service, due to their frail thin cables. I paid $25 for them at Walmart, and was pleased with their good sound quality, good sound isolation, and relative comfort. So, the hunt began for a replacement set of earbuds. Most headphones and earbuds I've owned before share that common death of the cabling breaking down and causing one ear or both to short out. First and foremost, I wanted good to excellent audio quality, and either durability or inexpensive and easily replaceable buds. I was gravitating toward the Klipsch S4's because of their many great reviews and the video review of them at CNET. However, there are a number of bad reviews here on Amazon for them, mostly complaining about the frail cables, lack of L shaped plug, and general lack of durability. I can't see paying $70 for a set of buds that might not even last a year. I use mine with my LG Thrive phone (or Sony MP3 player) to listen to MP3's or stream Pandora while mowing, laying out in the sun, or during my daily bicycle rides. Mowing often means the cords get snagged on low branches, which is hard on thin cords. After seeing these little beauties rated so highly, and absurdly inexpensive, I just had to try them. Just got them today, and I'm amazed at their sound quality for the price! I would have to say my Koss KE29's sounded marginally better, and had slightly more maximum volume. But, I could buy 5 sets of these Panasonic RPHJE120K's for what I paid for my Koss KE29's. Although, if you don't mind violet or pink, you can get the KE29's from Amazon for a bit over $9. The Panasonic RPHJE120K's are more comfortable than the KE29's, which is another very important criteria. What good is great sound if they hurt your ears, or won't seal properly? I ripped these out of the package and plugged them right into my ears with the medium sized ear tips that came installed, and they fit perfectly! I had to use the smallest tips on my KE29's, and the left bud would not always stay in my ear and seal perfectly. The medium tips were too big. The tips on these Panasonics are more supple, and conform to the ear better. Also the ergonomic shape of the buds themselves fit into the area around the ear canal and help support them, so that the eartips are not the only part of the bud that supports them in the ear. I may grab those Klipsch S4's someday if the price dips, or they come out with an improved model. I'm curious to see if the sound quality justifies the higher price. But, right now I'm very pleased with my under $6 purchase of these Panasonic RPHJE120K's! If you need good sound quality for cheap, because you tend to destroy earbuds with heavy use, these are for you!

title: Very nice that you would think of me on my 70th ...
body: Hi, Honey,Very nice that you would think of me on my 70th birthday-to-be. Yeah, THE big one. Or one of the big ones, anyway. I mean, they’re all big from here on out, right?I’d like to say I’ll definitely return your favor of thoughtful kindness, but I’m not sure. I mayn’t (lived over 69 years to use that once!) still be around in 31 years. (big joke)Thanks for “H is for Hawk,” I do look forward to reading it. Oh, and “H" really is for hawk, I looked it up. Or maybe Hillary? I read you young people are going forSanders… (another big joke)I signed your petition, but thought I’d see if I got anything before I told you, LOL. [final big joke]I do have this to offer for your own BIG ONE: I’m listening, right now, to Andante Spianato & Grande Polonaise in E flat major, op. 22, by Chopin, the original Polishprince (not Paul Anka, the usurper!!), and was reminded of a singular trip to San Francisco. We met. We ate. Hugo's. You dropped me at my hotel. I’m going to myroom, but hear what sounded like a quartet or chamber orchestra and wonder, could they be live? Yes! So I sat myself down in an off-lobby space to see themand to hear them. The chamber orchestra, as it turned out, finished their piece, stood up, we applauded, they left. Huh?Then, before I could gather my wits and go to bed, some punk black kid wearing a ball-cap and an attitude, hoisting a small gym bag, sat upon the bench of THESTEINWAY GRAND at center stage. The very nerve! OK, karaoke for pianists? So, he pulls out a pile of music, arranges it neatly upon the piano-top, selects awork, and carefully introduces me to Frederic Chopin. Over and over.He clearly loves this stuff, he slides across the keyboard, he slides across the piano bench. This was a rendition of classical music never heard or imagined by me.That young man stopped conversation in that room that night. We got it.I have no idea who that kid was. At some point I had to go to bed or catch a plane or whatever, but I would dearly love to thank that then young man for hisrepresentation of Chopin’s music for our audience. Point is, if I’m not around for your BIG ONE, go hear some Chopin and/or get these incredible ear buds*(Panasonic ErgoFit Earbuds RP-TCM125-K) and send Meg the bill. Oh, and if you find some 50-60 year old black man playing Chopin with authority and aball-cap, anywhere at all in the Bay Area, please give him what you can in my memory.*This is not an ad, cost me about $10, best ever, select a color, unique oval insert shape since none of us have round ear canals, go figger.

title: Panasonic RP-HJE120...Pleasantly Surprised
body: My favorite over-the-ear headphones finally became unusable when the foam started falling apart, so I started looking for another pair of similar headphones. While these type of headphones often have very full sound, the biggest complaint is that they're usually terribly hot to wear. Having used the over-the-ear type for years, I had to agree. So I started looking at in-ear phones instead.Prior to these, I've never owned a pair of in-ear headphones. I've had various earbuds, like the ones that come with the iPod, and I've always found those to be terribly uncomfortable, and poor sounding. So I started looking at true in-ear phones that are lightweight, cool to wear, and comfortable. I looked at ones up to $100, and I stumbled into the Panasonic RP-HJE120 phones by accident. Reading the glowing reviews, I naturally assumed that this was a subjective thing from people on very tight budgets, but I absent-mindedly put a pair of these $7 in my cart. I finally decided on a pair of Aluminum RHA MA-350 phones for about $35, and just left the Panasonics in the cart because they were so cheap.I got the 2 sets of headphones about 3 days apart, and I gave them each a quick listen. The HJE120s sounded ok, but rather tinny as I expected. The RHAs were much richer, with very clear highs, but again very little on the low end. So I decided to burn them in, as I've seen recommended by lots of people. I plugged them into my spare PC and played rock music on each of them for about 24 hours at medium-high volume.SOUND: After burning them in, the $7 Panasonics came to life. I discovered they have to be pushed into your ears a bit for there to be adequate bass, but the bass is dramatically better than out of the box. They have a fairly rich sound, but are a little weak on the highs.COMFORT: They come with 3 different earpieces, and I used the smallest one, which fit the best for me. They cause very little ear fatigue.CORD: The cord is just a little short. When my iPod is in my pocket, it sometimes pulls tight when I'm walking around doing yard work. It should be about 5" longer.QUALITY: This is something that can only be determined with time, and after only a couple weeks of owning them, they continue to work fine. They do feel just a little cheap, but they *are* very inexpensive, so that can't really be held against them. Besides, for headphones, lightweight is good!In conclusion, for my listening taste, the Panasonic RP-HJE120 simply blew away the RHA MA-350, and I can buy 5 pairs for the same price, which I will probably do, so I have some spares. What an awesome pleasant surprise!

title: 'BEST EVER'  AFFORDABLE  IN-EAR HEADPHONES
body: I needed a smaller headphone for my Kindle and my smartphone for holiday traveling, because the headphones I own are large and bulky. The only time I use my bulky headphones is when I have family guests and don't want my music to interfere with their quiet time. It occurred to me that the best option for traveling would be ear-buds. Yet, as a senior, I've never used ear-buds, although I've seen the younger generation using them almost exclusively.I recalled that there was a set of ear-buds included with my Android, however I never use them because I only use my smartphone for calls when away from home; certainly not for listening to music. So, the Android ear-buds were tossed aside, and I couldn't recall where I put them. I decided to do a bit of searching on Amazon to review which product would be best for my needs. I was not disappointed.Thanks to the thousands of positive reviews detailing the audio quality and technical specifications of the Panasonic RPHJE120K In-Ear Headphones I felt confident that this product would be the best choice for my traveling needs. My only reservation was my limited technical understanding of how 'quality audio' could be transmitted through such a 'thread-thin' cord, especially having never used ear-buds. I waited with anticipation, and positive expectation.It didn't take long for the Panasonic RPHJE120K In-Ear Headphones to arrive. Giddy with excitement, I tried the ear-buds on my laptop and was astonished by the audio quality! In fact, I could not discern a lot of difference in the audio quality between the expensive 'professional' bulky headphones my son purchased for me two years ago, and the Panasonic RPHJE120K In-Ear Headphones. I then tried out my Panasonic RPHJE120K In-Ear Headphones on my Android, accessing my MP3 playlist from the Amazon Cloud app on the smartphone. Again, I was absolutely amazed by the near excellent audio quality of these ear-buds, which cost LESS than the tax on my bulky large headphones.Ordinarily, I would have been highly skeptical of any product which cost a fraction of standard rates for comparable/ similar products. Yet, with the Panasonic RPHJE120K In-Ear Headphones you will get a great product offering excellent audio for a low cost. Even though I am not tech savvy, I promise. You will not be disappointed with this product if you need quality ear-buds. In fact, I was so pleased I purchased another Panasonic RPHJE120K In-Ear Headphone to have on hand if I misplace the original.If you are searching for new ear-buds, I recommend that you ... BUY THIS PRODUCT!!!Maizie Lucille JamesDecember 23, 2012

title: BEST ON THE MARKET, MY 2nd PAIR
body: THE BEST earbuds on the market for the price. Even if they were charging $50 for these, it would still be a deal. I know with all the other thousands of five star reviews, one more really isn't going to matter or put a dent in the tipping scale that has already tipped towards the side of excellence. However therein lies the reason for this review.This product is so great and unbelievable for the price, that one just can't sit quiet and not have the urge to praise it in some fashion. As many great reviewers before me have also stated, I have spent over $70 on pairs of headphones before and none come close to amazement when it comes to price. Usually you get what you pay for, but not here. Oh no, you get much more than what you pay for and they do amaze.If you are looking for a pair of earbuds that are great in all areas of the audio spectrum; lows, mids and highs. Then you need look no further, for these are the perfect balance. Crystal clear highs, good midrange and an amazement in lows considering these are only earbuds.Out of all the Japanese made products these days, I think Panasonic is one of the only ones left still making quality products. Even Sony has been heading down the cheap road for the past 10 years and I used to be big on Sony products, but I won't buy anything they make anymore because it's too touch and go. However, Panasonic still seems to deliver.I have a $90 pair of Panasonic headphones with a 50 hour break in period that don't sound much better than these earbuds after being broken in. Yes, you will need to break them in and surprisingly, being as tiny as they are, they actually do break in. They will not sound great right out of the package, but they won't sound bad either.After a few weeks of frequent use they will begin to break in and the sound will improve by almost 70%. My first pair lasted about 7 years before meeting their demise, due to a new kitten thinking that they were a toy. They went through the wash at least once and still worked as good as before they went in lol. If that happens to you, make sure to let them dry out for at the very least, 48 hrs and I swear they should still work. These earbuds really hold up.This is my second pair and I can only hope they last half as long as the first pair. As often as I use earbuds, If they last me two years, I've gotten my money's worth. I've even thought of buying a few more pairs to have on hand in case these ever become obsolete.Definitely an excellent investment and you couldn't possibly be disappointed for the price, I promise. I very highly recommend.

title: Most comfortable earbuds - greatest value - a clear no-brainer
body: To start, I seem to specialize in destroying earbuds. Certainly not intentionally, but, it happens. I cannot speak to the overall durability of these earbuds since I just received them. I'll post more on their durability; however, I did not want to wait to add a review of what I know of these, having owned them a short while.These earbuds are BY FAR the most comfortable earbuds I've ever owned. I work with earbuds in, and I prefer to either run the cord up my shirt (to keep from snagging it on a shovel handle or other obstacle) or behind my back.I have a few pairs of Ink'd buds and found myself CONSTANTLY pushing them BACK INTO my ears...totally inconvenient.These Panasonic earbuds have a unique (as I've seen) method to fit perfectly into the ear and protrude minimally, if at all. Important if you wear them with any sort of hat or hood that moves past your ears.The right angle plug seems sturdy, and having a right-angle plug helps to keep the snag-factor to a minimum. I normally break earbuds at the plug because...they get snagged and literally ripped out of my ears, and sometimes the mp3 player/ mobile phone I'm using...and it usually happens within a snap of time...so I work to minimize snags.The sonic range of the earbuds are OK. Highs seem well reproduced and Bass is not bad, BUT it's "honky"... over flush in mid-range tones that, while the situation can be mitigated with an EQ, it's how they come out of the package.For the price, I picked up 2 pairs to see how they will fare in my world.A serious value for the money, if only for the comfort! For what I need them for, they are likely to be the best I've owned. Cable is long enough, but not silly-long; and more importantly, NOT TOO SHORT.I picked up BLACK and PURPLE: [[ASIN:B003EM8008 B003EM8008]] photo of the PURPLE is a tad deceiving only because the LAVENDER cable is not apparent... and it's a girly-purple... not that there's anything wrong with that; just helpful to know ahead of time.Overall, incredible value, comfort and great sound. Run, walk, work, mow, string-trim, leaf-blow, kayak...whatever your favorite activity, these will stay with you to the point that you won't remember you're wearing them.Buy lots and share!EDIT: I have used these earbuds for a few weeks now and I'm very impressed. I forget I'm wearing them and I do not have to keep re-adjusting them to stay in my ears! I'll be buying more of these to have on hand for myself, for stocking stuffers, etc. This is a 'can't miss' product and price point!

title: Best earbuds I've ever owned, period.
body: These are hands-down the best earbuds I've ever owned. Tl;dr: they're insanely cheap, comfortable (even for side-sleeping), sound great, and durable as hell. I 100% recommend these. Read on for the longer version.I used to go through a pair of earbuds about every 9 months to a year. One ear would quit working, a cord would break or pull out, etc.. I'm not super careful with earbuds--I keep them in my pocket, occasionally drop them, get them damp, things like that. However, these buds lasted me a year and a half, working perfectly the whole time. The only reason I had to get a new pair is because my old ones got run over in a parking lot. And they still sort of worked! Despite being literally in pieces, sound came out of both buds and the remote button still worked. I was genuinely gutted when they broke because I was afraid they wouldn't still be available--thank god they are!Other than amazing durability, these are just all-around quality headphones.First, they're comfy. I regularly sleep (on my side) in them, and I almost never wake up with sore ears. They're also usually still in when I wake up in the morning. These are the first buds I've seen that had the actual speakers (like, the part inside the rubber tips) shaped to fit the ears. Most are circles, these are ovals. This would probably also make them comfy for small-eared folks.I'm no true audiophile, so I can't comment on the specifics of the sound quality. But for my purposes, the sound is great. It's crisp, the bass is pretty strong, and the volume range is good. The noise isolation is also pretty darn impressive. (These are noise-isolating, not noise-cancelling, to be clear.)Another little thing I find handy is the right-angled connector. My audio jack is on the bottom of my phone, so this lets me rest it on things without feeling like I'm breaking the cable. It also makes it harder to accidentally catch on things.The only negative thing I have to say about these is that I really wish the mic/remote had volume controls. It does have one "universal" button, so you can accept and end calls, pause music, skip tracks, etc., but it's annoying to have to pick up my whole phone to change the volume.I guess for some another negative might be the looks. They're very plain, plastic, not flashy. Personally, that doesn't matter much to me. And apparently they do offer some color options, if that's your thing.In sum, these are fantastic. For under $15, you really can't beat them. I'll definitely be repurchasing (if I ever need to).

title: Small-eared Rain-Man Girl loves these!
body: I bought these earbuds about a year ago, and buying earbuds is always a gamble for me. I go through them quickly because the way I use them is hard on them, but I also have very small ear canals that easily start to feel uncomfortable with earbuds in after a while if they aren't pretty small. That's not good; I have a nice little cornucopia of health conditions that mean that I tend to use my iPod as an aid in getting through my daily life, but because of a physical tic I have where I press my ears closed, I go through earbuds quickly because even hardier models get beat up over time from my ticcing. Therefore, I have to shop for earbuds that 1.) are small and comfortable enough to fit in my tiny ear canal and not cause discomfort over time, 2.) have decent, fairly noise-blocking sound to drown out the background noise I'm trying to block out, and 3.) be pretty cheap, because regardless of how well they're made, I'm going to go through them pretty quickly. It just doesn't make sense for me to buy expensive earbuds given these factors, and when I find something that works, I tend to stick with it, out of necessity more than anything else.So glad I took the gamble on these earbuds. I found them through an article about earbuds that were good for people with small ears, and ordered them off of Amazon about a year ago. Despite the price, after frequent use, they're only now BEGINNING to show the first stages of wearing out. That's impressive, given my use! And because they're not pricey, I can order another pair when I need to, the brand is common enough that I'm liable to find them easily, and hey, cute colors--orange is my favorite. The sound quality is what I need it to be--I'm not an audiophile, but I like my music to sound reasonably good, and these have not lost a whole lot of quality over the past year, even with my weird tics--and the smaller inserts or silicon cups included with the earbuds fit comfortably in my micro-ears for a good amount of time. I could probably go a while longer without having to replace them, but I'm mostly back here buying a new pair to have something to fill my cart and get free shipping...I'm serious! I'll keep using the pair I have now as long as I can, and might update this review with a total cumulative figure for the "lifespan" of my first pair before going into the second pair, for the sake of usefulness. I'm happy to find that they're still here on Amazon and only a dollar or two higher in price than previously..looks like I have a supplier for my earbuds into the long-term now! Thanks, Panasonic!

title: Great value for price
body: I read some reviews for the best budget headphones and one of the picks was the Panasonic RPTCM125A Headphones. It was the lowest price among all the competitors so naturally I had some reservations but those faded away pretty quickly.PriceThe price is really on the low side. You don't see many products at this price range without sacrificing either quality or style. For $15 there's really no need to be protective of these. They're cheap enough that if you lose or break a pair; no worries, just buy new ones. I constantly just wrap these up and place them in my pockets without a second thought. (DON'T DO THESE WITH EXPENSIVE PAIRS)ShippingI placed the order on November 1st and had it by the 24th. Incredibly quick shipping with no damage in transit.PackagingOkay packaged, at this price range what can one expect. Comes with the headphones (duh) and two other size sets of earbuds (large and small). The medium size is placed on.FitThe fit is really nice for these earbuds. When I try on Apple products or others in general, I find them falling out constantly but with these they're great. I've exercised and gone to the gym and without fail they've stayed on.Sound QualityWhen I paid $15 I didn't expect studio quality and I didn't get it. When I first placed them in my ears I didn't have high hopes or expectations but I was pleasantly surprised at the sound. There's no thunderous bass or crystal clear highs but good enough for me. I have quality at headphones I use at home but when I'm on the go, I use these. Like I said bass isn't mind blowing but it's decent. If you're looking for bass heavy headphones these aren't the pair you want. Mids perform pretty well and the highs are actually good for $15. If you're like me and like to listen to the classics in the 70s-90s or current day pop; the Panasonic headphones are a great choice.Noice cancellationThere's obviously no active noise cancellation on these but if you put the volume at around half, you can easily drown out all outside noise; great for when pesky strangers are being too loud on the subway.Overall these earbuds aren't for everyone. If you're on a budget and want some decent headphones without breaking the bank, these are a great choice and far better than any Apple headphone. However, if you're an audiophile or looking for bass heavy response, steer clear from the Panasonic headphones and go for Skullcandys'. Well that's just my two cents.

title: Initially OK, we'll see how long these last UPDATED
body: I just got these and ran them through my standard test set plus a couple others for new headphones:Starz-Rock 6 times (70's Rock)Starz-Cherry BabyBaker Gurvitz Army - People (70's Rock)Mussorsky-Gates of Kiev from Pictures at an ExhibitionCopland-Promise of Living (Orchestral version from Atlanta Symphony)Holst-Jupiter from the PlanetsRelient K -Apathetic Way to BeDave Beagle-Joy (Jesu Joy of Man's Desiring, Acoustic Guitar)For new headphones, we're looking at basic sound quality. These are OK. They're a bit lacking on the bass and lower mid range, which gives them the impression that they're a bit "thin". You don't feel like you're getting the full sound of the recorded music. It's not awful, just average. It also seems like I'm having to crank up the volume to get a full sound feeling, thus I suspect that they're a bit inefficient (probably drag your battery down slightly faster).Here's my rating (out of 5) for various primary qualities of headphones:Fit=4Sound=3Build Quality=2Value=4I'm giving them an initial 1 star bump to 4 stars based on sound for the price. The build quality seems a bit lacking, but that will be determined by how long they last. My goal for ear buds is a year, although most of them don't make it that long. I'll try to remember to update this review when they do go.I bought them because of the huge amount of favorable reviews and low price point, lets hope all those people were right.UPDATE: 10/15/14 I started using these a little over a year ago and I've just retired them. The left side driver has started to drop significantly in volume and overall, the sound level versus volume setting on my ipod has decreased compared to the new set I just openned up. This seems to be a common problem with all earbuds, one side's volume will eventually fall off significantly. Still, they lasted over a year, at a very cheap price, which is way better than some of highly praised competition. In the above review I denegrated the overall build quality. I was wrong, these have been much tougher than they looked. The cord and plug has stood up to those annoying yanks from door knobs, bike handle bars or various tools they seem to get caught on when I'm working.Am I satisfied? Well, I openned up a new set this morning and I need to put another set on order as my next backup.

title: The best bang for you buck - bar none
body: Let me start off by saying that I have used many, many different earbuds and headphones over the years, and these are by FAR the best bang for my buck in terms of quality, durability, and comfort. I've always managed to land jobs where I'm able to listen to audio content throughout my workday, and have consequently spent most of my working hours with at least one earbud tucked into my ears.I thread these down to my pocket where they are plugged into my iPod full time. No matter how much running, walking, lifting, jumping or moving around I do (and I work as a package delivery guy, which means hundreds of movements and ten miles of walking a day), these things take ages to wear out. Under normal use, I'm sure they'd never need replacing unless you managed to blow out the speaker somehow, or cause blatant damage to them. They don't cut in and out from wear on the jack end or on the headphone ends like some other earbuds, which will give way after being cranked around a bit.Next, I have to address the comfort of the ergonomically shaped buds themselves. They are formed to fit into ear, with the speaker being offset from the center of the housing. On others, such as the classic white Apple earbuds, the round shape causes me some discomfort and soreness after hours of wear. But these are just fantastic. I wear them for up to 11 hours at a time with no discomfort whatsoever.Finally, the sound quality. While an audiophile purist will say that they probably don't meet the highest standards in the land, they do a pretty good job. They stand up to much more expensive earbuds, and if you use the proper sized adapter for your ear (three sizes included), the speaker isolates sound very well, so you don't even need any noise-canceling feature. I wear them while driving a diesel truck with the doors open driving down the freeway, and I can hear every word of the audiobook I'm listening to. The treble is good. The bass is excellent for such an inexpensive earbud. And the overall sound quality is going to put other in-ear buds to shame.For less than ten bucks, there is no better ear bud out there. I've owned three pairs over the last two years, and I would only have needed one if I didn't abuse them so much with daily use and extreme wear. If you want to try these, but would like to keep a microphone feature for hands-free calls, you can get some identical to these with in-line microphone in the Panasonic RPTCM125K model.

title: Excellent Quality for the Price!
body: This product, for it's price point, has considerably nice quality, both in sound and construction.I originally bought a green pair (because it was the only color on sale at the time) to try these out and I ended up loving them.The first thing I noticed was how nicely it fits in the ear.It's snug, but not uncomfortable by any means.You do have to make sure that you're putting the correct bud in the proper ear, of course, or else they'll be uncomfortable.They're angled a bit so that you can't wear the left bud in the right ear or the right bud in the left ear - this makes for a better fit overall, though.I came back and bought a couple more pairs (in black, orange, and blue) after my wife and I began fighting over who got the "good earbuds" while working out at the gym together.Now, these aren't $50 earbuds, so when I say the quality is good, I mean it's notably good considering the price.I like how the fit of these seems to help keep outside noises out - such as people running on the treadmill, for example - and allows me to just lose myself to my music.The sound quality is pretty good - not "tinny" like many cheap earbuds tend to be.I heard some people complain about the lack of base, but I didn't really notice to be honest - maybe because the music I listen to isn't super base heavy? If you're listening to dub-step, I suppose these wouldn't be the best for that.I listen to mostly alternative rock and pop, and I've had no issues with feeling that any part of the sound quality was lacking in any real, noticeable way.Beyond the great fit and good quality, the price is probably the biggest selling point here.For about $8 a pair, this is a real steal.The only thing left to do is to see how long they hold up in the long run.Plus, I like the selective range of bright colors - makes spotting them 10x easier when you could have SWORN you put them right there, but of course you didn't, haha. Plus they have orange, my favorite color, so yanno. Little biased there. Just a little.Final line, if you're wearing these on a plane, bus, out jogging, or at the gym, these are pretty great.If you're used to $50+ earbuds in general, these might not be for you.If you're a gamer and rely on pinpoint sound accuracy, these also may not be for you.Everyone else - fair game.

title: Sounds great and then some.
body: After purchasing most of the budget earphones (since I am a college student on a minimal budget) found on Amazon, this pair has to be the best buy. These in-ear headphones challenge top-tier in-ears, even the well-respected Klipsch S4's. The sound for these $8 dollar headphones are simply outstanding, with the bass loud and thumping and the treble clear and crisp. The treble is clearer than the S4's but the bass is not as present and warm, but nonetheless still "banging". This pair is worth more than it is priced and therefore serves as a great pick for budget-given audiophiles. The praise given for these small but wonderful headphones cannot be explained but experienced since it truly is hard to believe that this pair can rival a $100 high-end headphone. Although the build is not as sturdy, with the cord constantly tangled and twisted and the era pieces being plastic, the sound more than compensates for the build quality. Despite this, these in-ear headphones/earbuds have lasted me for more than 5 months (and counting), keeping in mind that it was not used daily but majority of that time, at about 75% volume. If you've taken an interest in this product or are looking for high-quality sound in-ear headphones, these are your pair.Here's a comparison of in-ear headphones I've owned:Rating 1-10 (Worst-Best)Panasonice RPHJE120K:Overall Sound: 9Treble: 9Bass: 8.5Voice Clarity:9Build Quality: 7Klipsch S4-II:Overall: 9Treble:8.5Bass:9Voice Clarity:8.5Build Quality: 9Klipsch S4-I:Overall: 9Treble: 8.5Bass: 9Voice Clarity:8.5Build Quality: 7.5SoundMagic E10:Overall:9.5Treble:9Bass:9.5Voice Clarity:9Build Quality: 7.5(First 2 weeks, left channel shorts/cuts it jack isn't plugged in a certain position)iLuviEP322SIL City LightsOverall:8Treble: 7Bass: 8.5Voice Clarity: 7Build Quality:7.5Sony: MDR-EX210BOverall 8.5Treble:8Bass:8.5Voice Clarity:8Build Quality:8.5Pioneer:SE-CL721-KOverall:8.5Treble:8.5Bass:9Voice Clarity:8Build Quality:9 (circa)I have personally used these headphones and typically listen to R&B (i.e: Miguel, Maxwell, D'angelo, Musiq, J.Cole, Kanye, Common)

title: Good for the price! No regrets
body: Durability 5/5: Bought these almost 4 years ago and they have lasted me extremely well. I had a pair of Samsung earbuds that came with my phone about 3 years ago and they eventually died about 2 1/2 years after regular usage. These earbuds, however, are very hardy and durable. I wrap them up a little too tightly, I admit, and there is absolutely no sign of wear and tear on the cord. The in-ear part is also very sturdy, taking a reasonable amount of jostling, and so far so good.Model 3/5: It did take a LOT of getting used going from the old, traditional earbuds to the in-ear style. I have terrible sinuses and so I will never truly get over how they suction into your ear (and when they get ripped out after having a good suction...AWFUL!!). Because of the in-ear, suctiony-ness, you will also get cord noise. As in, every time that you touch the cord or it rubs up against something, it will be very obvious and amplified. this is unpleasant until you get used to ignoring it. All of these things seem less about quality, and more just about getting used to this kind of earbud.Sound 5/5: I'm not an audiophile by any means, but these earbuds have a really strong bass kick! Quite good sound for the price!!! Not tinny at all, not muted at all, not scratchy at all. I'm VERY impressed with the audio that these cheap little things can push out!---Edit: Compared to higher end over-the-head headphones, I can tell that the bass is definitely boosted and the mids to highs are diminished. However, the overall sound is still clear. It's crystal clear sound, but with heavy base and the absence of some higher tones. You won't notice that the highs and some mids are missing unless you're listening to stuff with heavier emphasis on those registers. But if you're really into strong bass, these will definitely do the job.Comfort 4/5: Once you get used to the in-ear aspect, these are actual pretty comfortable. I ended up taking the rubber ends off and putting on different ones that fit into my ear better. However, the actual shape of the earbud, being slightly angled, allows it to rest in the ear quite comfortably. Just don't lay on your side with your ear squished into the pillow or that will be uncomfortable really fast.Overall: 5/5. I won't take off stars for my discomfort due to going inside the ear canal and then suctioning there because that is just how this kind of earbud works.

title: Best pair of earbuds you'll find for under $25
body: Yes I know what that these headphones are 10 bucks, but I still stand by that title. Let me explain why.Now, I'm not one to review stuff on Amazon, but I've been so satisfied by these earbuds that I felt I should give back in some way. I've owned 3 pairs of these earbuds over the past 5ish years and I will tell you my experience with them.First off, I'm going to give a little background with my use with earbuds. I am really rough with them. I accidentally step on them, roll them over with my chair, shove them in my backpack where they get squished, etc. Because of this, I used to go through a pair of earbuds every 1-2 months. I never put much research into them because I knew they weren't going to last very long. I usually picked the <$10 earbuds because that was all I could afford as a high school student with no job. Once I decided I should invest in a nicer $25 pair so that they would last me longer, but those only lasted me about 3 months. Because of this, I decided to stick with my cheap $10 earbuds.Now this is when I bought this set of earbuds. I wasn't expecting anything out of the ordinary, I just saw they had it in orange, so I got that pair. I had these headphones for 6 months and they were working perfectly fine by the end of it. The only reason why they broke was because my dog had destroyed themIn August of 2016, I decided to get the same pair again, but this time in blue. I had moved away to college by this point so I didn't have any dogs to deal with so I thought I'd give this pair another go. This pair lasted me all the way until December 2017 when they finally gave out. I was incredibly impressed with this! I still went back to using random earbuds for awhile, but none of them lasted nearly as long as this pair did.I decided to get get the same pair again but only in red. They arrived in December 2017 and only just now (April 2020) they are starting to give out. The left ear stopped working, so I decided to get my 4th pair. Today I went to my order history and I was so surprised at how long I had them for. I have to say, I don't know what they do to these earbuds, but they are the most durable that I have experienced.This pair is my go to earbuds that I bring with me everywhere I go, whether that is school, travel, or vacations. If you want a set of earbuds that will last, this is that pair.

title: Very nice, even at a higher price.
body: After going through 2 pairs of Ultimate Ears Superfi 5's and a comparable pair of Audio Technica's I decided no more high dollar headphones. They all eventually break. I listen to music at least 6 hours a day and it absolutely must sound good. The kind of music I listen to varies from progressive metal to thrash and everything in between. I purchased 5 pairs of ~$6 headphones and compared. Here they are, in the order that I'd rate them, strictly based on sound quality.Note: Anyone who is familiar with canal headphones knows that fit is everything. I took fit out of the equation by using my Comply T-400 foam tips with all of them. I highly recommend them if you want the best fit. I was also using a Sony MW600 bluetooth adapter, Xperia Tipo, and PowerAmp.This review is about sound quality only. Nothing else matters..* [[ASIN:B004PEIG12 iLuv iEP322BLK City Lights In-Ear Earphones - Ultra Bass - Black]]Could possibly be the best <$10 headphone out there. At first I thought they may be too boomy but after adjusting the eq a bit I fell in love. It's hard to believe you can get something this sweet sounding for 6 bucks. Nice and flat but a little heavy on the mid bass (nothing the eq cant cure. Handles high volumes very well.* [[ASIN:B003EM800S Panasonic RPHJE120S In-Ear Headphone, Silver]]These were practically a tie for first. I think they probably have the most accurate overall sound. I love thundering bass drums, but if I were seeking the best overall reproduction, these would have been #1.* [[ASIN:B001GS8FZ2 JBuds J2 Premium Hi-FI Noise Isolating Earbuds Style Headphones (Onyx Black)]]Decent, but lacks power at the ends of the frequency spectrum, good midrange. Very much worth what I paid, and I would probably have been happy with them if I hadn't had the 2 above to compare them to.* [[ASIN:B005FDOG6G JVC HAFX30B Headphone, Marshmallow, Black]]Also worth it's price twice over. Listened to these for a couple weeks when my AT's broke. Paid $14.88 for then locally, and felt that price was fair. Nothing spectacular, but they work in a pinch..* [[ASIN:B005FDOVPM JVC HAFX8B Headphone, Riptidz, In-Ear]]Didn't care for these much. They have a tin can sound I cant make right with the eq. Not recommended.There you have it.. One audiophiles opinion..

title: Cute little headphones
body: If you are In the market for a decent pair of wireless headphones at an Incredible price, I advise you to look no further. You can't beat the quality of these headphones compared to what your getting for the price. They are great to use for so many purposes, such as outdoors, Indoors, exercising, mobile calling, and more. Overall sound quality Is really nice. Not once did I have any Issues when listening to music or voicing with someone on a phone call. It was loud, clear, and easy to understand what was being said. I especially like the fact that I could easily answer calls or skip songs right from the comfort of the headphones. Something I wish more wireless headphones would Incorporate Into their products.I'm generally the type of person whom Isn't too fond of In-ear headphones. They usually aggravate me from being too loose, too tight, or not comfortable after short periods of times. I can't say that at all about this brand. You get several different size ear pieces that help make these headphones fit comfortable In your ears. They are designed with what I would call a hook design where they sit snugly In your ears, but are not fully submersed In the ear cavity. Your ears don't feel like their plugged up or have that "full" feeling, as I would sometimes get with most In-ear headphones. Luckily, with this brand, I haven't had that experience and don't think I will.Charge time Is quite short for these. I was charged and ready to go within an hour. Run time varied depending upon how I used them and how often I charged them. Under most circumstances, I charge them about twice a week and use them roughly 2 hours a day. Bluetooth syncing Is quite easy to do and not once have I run Into an Issue doing so. I noticed when these were paired with my iPhone 6, I was able to see a battery meter Icon for the headphones. It was super easy to check and see how much power my headphones had when using them In conjunction with my phone. I can't say the same for some of the Android devices I paired with. When pairing these with my Moto E phone, I didn't see the battery meter Icon and didn't have any way of knowing how much power I had left.This may vary depending upon different Android devices, but really unsure. Hopefully, It's something that will be Incorporated In the near future. It's a great feature that Is quite handy to have and use.

title: Best Beat for Your Buck
body: These headphones by Panasonic somehow out compete many similar products that are up to 250% more expensive. Unlike many headphones like Beats by Dre, these in ear headphones do not use gimmicks like adding metal plates or fancy wire coatings to artificially make their product feel luxurious and sell well. Panasonic ErgoFit In-Ear Earbud Headphones at a price of $8.00 were made with the practical person in mind. In short, they are made cheap, functionally, and comfortably. I based this five-star rating off how these headphones compare to in ear headphones in the price range of $5:00 - $20.00 by analyzing the in-ear feel, durability, and sound quality.Of these three criteria, a comfortable experience means the most for me. I use headphones very often at work, school, and at home. Tragically, one of two things happen when using headphones regardless of price. Either they hurt after fifteen minutes of use or they fall out every few minutes. However, these Panasonic headphones slide in comfortably and stay. I have worn these headphones for 4+ hours straight without pain or discomfort. They even stay in while running.When buying anything cheap, a common fear is the product will break quickly. My first thought after holding these headphones, which have the cheapest plastic two strand coating around their wiring, was that it would last a month at most. Last week, I had to buy these headphones for the second time in the past two years. Not because they broke, but because I lost my last pair. I tend associate the durability of headphones by the quality of chord they come with. This chord is light, flimsy, and looks like it came from the 90's, yet these headphones last for years.The only reason I ever consider buying more expensive headphones is for better sound quality. Going back and forth from different headphones, it is clear headphones at higher prices sound better. However, I do not think this discrepancy is worth the increase in price nor the decrease in comfort.Overall, these headphones are a successful feat by Panasonic. They manage to sell an amazing product with a focus on functionality in a market dominated by the eye test. I highly encourage anyone looking for a good pair of simple headphones to try the affordable, durable, and comfortable Panasonic ErgoFit In-Ear Earbud Headphones.

title: Best Earphones on the Market!!!! Get These If On A Budget!
body: As soon as I'm finished typing this review, I'm going to open my THIRD pack of these earphones. This will be important later. These earphones are the best earphones on the market. What I look for in earphones is simply the following: decent material, comfort, sound quality, price, and longevity.Decent Material: PASS. I'm not keen on material looks and feels, as long as it keeps everything together, but it's better than expected. From a first look, they definitely look like cheap earphones, but everything is kept together really well.Comfort: PASS WITH FLYING COLORS. These are the most comfortable earphones I've ever used. They're engineered to be that way. The eartips are at an angle, rather than straight, and it's at the right angle. I've tried bluetooth earphones made in a similar way, at various price ranges, and NONE of them match this level of comfort.Sound Quality: WHOA!!!!!!! These earphones sound fantastic. For the price, they surprised me. I'd be hard-pressed to find another set of headphones that sound like this for no more than $30 or $50. The best way that I can explain how these sound is comparing them to the UrBeats. Take away a third of the bass from the UrBeats and replace it with clarity. That's what these ErgoFit's sound like. They're that darn good.Price: I think that's self-explanatory. When the price fluctuates between $7 and $11, what do you think?!Longevity: Remember the very first thing I said in this review? If not, go back to that, then come back. I opened my first pack of these in November 2016. I opened my second pack in October of 2017. I'm about to open my THIRD pack today, in September of 2018. I'm averaging 10 or 11 months of use out of these earphones, which is INSANE. There are so many different kinds of earphones out there, but there isn't one that can last this long. I've owned so many pairs of earphones, ranging in price between $5 and $100 over the past decade or so. They've all averaged a length of ONE to THREE months. I'm getting way more use out of the ErgoFit's for an extremely affordable price, and it doesn't make sense that Panasonic figured this out several years ago and other companies haven't!Overall, these earphones are GREAT, and I can't recommend them enough! I don't plan on buying anything else but these.
